title: 🥟 Rally Rissol: A Taste of Porto with DSPT
description:
Data Science Portugal (DSPT) is teaming up with Data Makers Fest for a special gathering on the first evening of the conference — and this time, we’re taking it to the streets.

Join us for a food crawl through Porto: a relaxed, social walk where we’ll explore some of the city’s most emblematic cafés and restaurants while tasting one of Portugal’s most beloved classics — the *rissol*.

If you want to meet fellow data people while discovering Porto off the beaten path, this is the activity for you! Come ready to share conversations, ideas and discover the best *rissol* in Porto.

We’ll move as a group through a route of local spots, making short stops to try different variations. The tour will end at **Hard Rock Cafe**, right on time for the Data Makers Fest Happy Hour (you must have a conference ticket to attend this one).

**👟 Practical notes:**

* Expect light walking between stops (comfortable shoes recommended!)
* Bring some cash/card for food at each stop
* Come hungry — and curious!

Whether you're visiting Porto for the first time or you think you already know it well, this is a chance to experience the city in the best way we know: through **food and community**.

title: DSPT x Devoteam: AI Agents on the Run
description:
🌸 **Lisbon**, let's talk AI Agents! 🤖✨ **DSPT - Data Science Portugal** is thrilled to kick off the **Primavera AI Fest**, an exclusive four-part series bringing together the brightest minds in the local AI tech community.

***Check out the full Primavera AI Fest agenda [here](https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7449770982128381955/)***

For our very first event, we are teaming up with **Devoteam**, who will be hosting us at their Lisbon offices. Expect an evening packed with deep-dive technical sessions featuring experts from **Devoteam** and **QuintoAndar**, followed by high-level networking, food, and drinks.
Whether you're building GenAI pipelines or figuring out how to push agents to production, this is where you need to be.

***

### 🎙️ The Sessions

**Talk 1: Preserving History with AI: A RAG Journey Through 100+ Years of Football Archives** *By Rita Ribeiro (Devoteam)* How do you make a century of history instantly searchable? Rita will dive into a real-world Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) project developed by Devoteam for a major football club museum. Discover how they tackled the challenge of analyzing and surfacing insights from over 100 years of historical archives.

**Talk 2: Evaluating AI Agents at QuintoAndar** *By Lucas Cardoso & Rodolfo Abreu (QuintoAndar)* Still relying on "vibe checks" to see if your AI agents actually work? Stop guessing and start measuring. Dive into QuintoAndar's framework for evaluating AI agents—covering everything from golden datasets and the role of evals from experimentation to production. Learn how to turn GenAI unpredictability into reliable, production-ready performance.

***

### 🗓️ The Agenda

* **17:30** — 🚪 Reception & Check-in
* **18:00** — 👋 Welcoming by DSPT
* **18:10** — ⚽ *Session 1:* Preserving History with AI by Rita Ribeiro, Devoteam
* **18:30** — 📈 *Session 2:* Evaluating AI Agent by Lucas Cardoso & Rodolfo Abreu, QuintoAndar
* **18:50** — 🎤 Q&A
* **19:15** — 🍻 Networking, Drinks & Bites

***

### 📍 The Details

* **When:** April 29 at 5:30 PM
* **Where:** Devoteam Lisbon Office

Space is limited, so secure your spot, bring your curiosity, and let’s talk AI. See you there! 👋

title: DSPT x Data Makers Fest - A Vibe Coding Party
description:
**Data Science Portugal (DSPT)** is teaming up with **Data Makers Fest** for a special warm-up event ahead of the conference: a **Vibe Coding Party** focused on **creativity**, **GenAI**, and **development with code agents**.

This event is for people who enjoy building things, experimenting with new tools, and solving real problems in creative ways 💡

**🧠 What’s going to happen?**

Over the course of a full day, participants will:
• Take part in a **hands-on workshop** on **Vibe Coding** and **code agents**
• Work in teams to tackle a **practical AI / Data Science challenge**
• Use **GenAI tools** to speed up development and explore creative approaches
• **Pitch** their final solution in a short presentation
• **Vote** for the winning project 🎉

**🏆 The winning team will receive tickets to Data Makers Fest!**

**👥 Who is this event for?**

You don’t need prior experience with GenAI tools or vibe coding. Curiosity and a willingness to experiment are the most important requirements 💪

You can:
• Sign up with friends in **teams of 2–3 people**, or
• **Join on your own**, and we’ll help you form a team on the day.

**🗓️ Agenda**
• 10:45 — Check-in & welcome
• 11:00 — Vibe Coding Workshop
• 12:30 — Lunch & networking + team formation
• 13:45 — Challenge and tools presentation
• 14:00 — Team development session
• 17:00 — Project pitches
• 18:00 — Public vote & winner announcement

**📌 Spots are limited** — secure your place, join us for this Vibe Coding Party, and get a chance to win tickets to one of the coolest AI conferences around!

title: Women in AI & Data - An "Ask Me Anything"-style Roundtable on AI careers
description:
In celebration of **International Women’s Day**, we are hosting a special event dedicated to **celebrating Portuguese women working in Artificial Intelligence and Data**. Our goal is to highlight role models in the AI ecosystem, while also **inspiring girls and young women** to explore these fields, build confidence, and envision themselves in technical and leadership roles.

Designed for **students, early-career professionals, and anyone interested in AI, Data, and technology**, this is a **safe, open, and welcoming space** for honest conversations about careers in AI. By bringing together women at different stages of their professional journeys, we aim to promote **diversity, equality, and inclusion**, and to help bridge the confidence and representation gap that still exists in the tech industry.

**️ Speakers**

**Clara Matos** — *Director of Applied AI at Sword Health*

Clara leads Applied AI at Sword Health, where she builds and scales machine learning systems that directly impact patient care at a global scale. With a background in computer vision, human motion analysis, and large-scale AI product development, her work sits at the intersection of AI, product, and engineering, with a strong focus on real-world impact.

**Filipa Castro** — *Head of AI Tech Lab at Euronext*

Filipa leads the development and deployment of AI systems in production within a highly regulated environment at Euronext. With experience spanning computer vision, traditional machine learning, and generative AI, she brings a unique perspective on scaling AI responsibly while bridging research, product strategy, and organizational change.

**Mariana Fonseca** — *CEO & Co-Founder of JuniorAI*

Mariana is a BSc student in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science at the University of Porto and the CEO and co-founder of JuniorAI, a student-led AI enterprise focused on building impactful, data-driven solutions. As a young leader in AI, she represents the growing role of student innovation, entrepreneurship, and early leadership in shaping the future of the field.

**Location**

**Euronext Tech Center Porto**
Rua de Aníbal Cunha 218, 4050-047 Porto

**Schedule**

* 16:00 — Check-in & Welcome
* 16:15 — **Women in AI & Data**: AMA-style roundtable with the speakers
* 17:15 — Coffee Break & Networking
* 18:00 — Closing

Whether you are a student taking your first steps in AI, a professional navigating your career, or an ally who wants to support a more inclusive tech ecosystem, **this event is for you**! Join us for an **open conversation**, **meaningful connections**, and an inspiring **celebration of women shaping the present and future of AI**.

title: DSPT x Devscope: All about AI Agents
description:
*DSPT presents*
**From Development to Evaluations - All about AI Agents**

AI Agents are transforming the way we build software, automate workflows, and design intelligent systems. In this meetup, we’ll dive into both sides of agent development: building powerful GenAI-driven workflows and ensuring they are trustworthy, measurable, and robust.

**Coding in the Age of Agents, by [Rui Quintino](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rquintino/)**
Using the latest coding agents like Claude Code and GitHub Copilot, we’ll explore how GenAI and agentic loops are changing the way we build software. Are these just “stochastic parrots”… or something more? And in this new stack, what role is left for us?

**Your Agent needs some Evals, by [Pedro Azevedo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pedro-azevedo-/)**
Developing robust GenAI agents is best done with Eval Driven Development (EDD). In this session you'll learn how you can implement EDD by making evaluation a first-class citizen throughout your development cycle. We will cover the practical steps of defining relevant metrics and building comprehensive test sets for GenAI's and LLMOps unique challenges. Crucially, we'll demonstrate how to leverage open-source tools like MLflow to streamline this process, enabling you to track, compare, and version evaluation results alongside your models for truly reproducible and data-driven AI development.

**Agenda**
18h00: Get together
18h15: Talk: "**Your Agent needs some Evals"**, with **Pedro Azevedo**
18h45: Coffee Break + Networking
19h15: Talk: "**Coding in the Age of Agents**", with **Rui Quintino**
20h00: Closing remarks

Can make it in person? We'll share a link for participants who want to join online **🤝**
Reserve your spot today and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

title: DSPT x NOS: AI in Action - From Global Data to Local Impact
description:
Lisbon, we've arrived! 🚀 Join Data Science Portugal for our comeback to the capital on **October 1st**, hosted by our friends at **NOS**, with a **special guest from Feedzai**.

NOS will open the doors of their Lisbon office for us to **meet, greet and learn about the latest in AI innovation**. We'll explore one of the biggest challenges in Data Science today: how do you adapt an AI model trained on an original dataset to a new market?

**Ricardo Pereira** from **Feedzai** will present **"Hey, I've Seen This One!"**, where he'll show how **transfer learning techniques** are applied to augment fraud detection systems. By leveraging knowledge across different markets and products, these methods help models adapt faster, spot attacks earlier, and reduce retraining costs. The talk will cover practical considerations for deploying these techniques in production, as well as ongoing research that will shape the next generation of fraud prevention. Don't miss it!

You will also get an inside look at data innovation at the heart of NOS. The expert team from **NOS** will share three cutting-edge projects:

* **The Journey of AI at NOS:** Diogo Passagem Santos will walk through the evolution of Data Science at the company, from classic on-premise machine learning projects to modern, cloud-based generative AI assistants.
* **NOS Recsys:** Football is a beloved sport both at the stadium and at home. Tiago Monteiro will explain how this AI-driven NOS recommender system tailors sports channel offers for fans, considering complex market dynamics and viewing patterns.
* **Guia-NOS:** Joana Sousa will present a mobile application that uses computer vision and GenAI to assist blind people. This project was piloted at NOS Alive, and showed huge potential for enabling more independent and secure navigation of the environment.

**📅 Event Agenda:**

* **16:45 - 17:00:** Meet & Greet
* **17:00 - 17:55:** NOS Talks + Q&A
* **17:55 - 18:25:** Coffee Break & Networking
* **18:25 - 19:10:** "Hey, I've Seen This One!" - Talk by Ricardo Ribeiro Pereira (Feedzai) + Q&A
* **19:10 - 19:30:** Final Networking

Don't miss this chance to learn from the best and reconnect with your peers. We're excited to be back in Lisbon and can't wait to see you there!

title: Meetup #120 - Leveraging Machine Learning for Industrial Process Digitalisation
description:
Welcome to another DSPT meetup at Aveiro! 🙌
=== SCHEDULE ===
• 18:30 - 18:45: Get together
• 18:45 - 18:55: Welcome message
• 19:00 - 19:40: Talk + Q&A: **"Leveraging Machine Learning for Industrial Process Digitalisation – Exploring a Real-World Industrial Use Case"** by **José Cação**
• 19:40 - 20:30: Networking/Coffee Break
• 20:30: Dinner is optional, but might be an excellent networking opportunity! 🍕register [here](https://forms.gle/k6ZqH9AuUucphpEJ7).
A special thanks to our venue IT 👏

# See you there!

**Abstract:** Currently, the manufacturing process of digitalisation is a major focus within the industrial paradigm. Particularly, Machine Learning (ML) is seen as a crucial driver for industrial process automation and optimisation, providing a promising alternative to traditional, less flexible rule-based industrial data processing and analysis methods. This presentation explores a real-world industrial application of integrating ML within industrial fault detection and diagnosis tasks, developed in collaboration with Bosch Termotecnologia S.A. The use case involves an end-of-line testing procedure conducted for all produced boilers, one major bottleneck in this equipment’s production lines. More specifically, an innovative, data-driven methodology to detect and classify potential faults within the spark ignition process, exclusively analysing electrical signal data. The proposed methodology entails a complex data preparation and pre-processing procedure, adapted to the current production infrastructure, combined with a two-step classification approach which (1) separates normal and abnormal signals (binary classification) and (2) classifies the potential type of fault (multilabel classification). Results showcase improvements in up to 80% in detection accuracy over current knowledge-based approaches implemented by the partner, with the two-step approach outperforming traditional single-stage multilabel classification by up to 10%.

***Bio:***
José Cação holds a master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Aveiro (2023, final grade 18/20), having finished his thesis in 2023 with the highest 20/20 grade. The work focused on optimising industrial equipment energy consumption through Deep Learning (DL) approaches. Currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering at the University of Aveiro, José Cação focuses his research on industrial data analysis, process digitalisation, and optimisation through Machine Learning (ML) and DL approaches, and the use of Explainable AI to improve model interpretability, trust, and auditability in AI-based decision-making systems.
Throughout his academic career, he has been actively involved in applied research projects with Bosch Termotecnologia S.A., mainly through the Augmanity and ILLIANCE projects – the latter of which he currently contributes to as a researcher. José Cação has authored and co-authored multiple publications, including articles in peer-reviewed journals like Sensors and presentations at leading international conferences, earning distinctions such as the “Best Paper Award” – 5th International Conference on Industry 4.0 and Smart Manufacturing (ISM 2023) and “Best Oral Presentation” at TechMA 2024, at the University of Aveiro.

**☀️ DSPT AI on Tap #1 – Gradient Descent your day into Happy Hour 🍻**

The sun is shining, and it’s the perfect time to take our AI conversations outside!
Join us for the first **DSPT AI on Tap**—a relaxed, informal gathering of AI and data science enthusiasts at Catraio's sunny garden over some beers!
Whether you're deep into machine learning, just curious about AI, or simply looking to meet great people in the community, this is your chance to unplug from your screen and connect IRL.

**💬** **Expect**:
**•** Casual networking
• Cold drinks & warm vibes
• No talks, no slides—just people, ideas, and (AI-themed) games!

**📅 When**: May 21st
🕓 **Time**: 6pm
📌 **Location**: Catraio - Craft Beer Shop & Bar

Grab a drink, pull up a chair, and let’s talk AI in the sun. ☀️🍺
RSVP now!

title: From analytics to narratives. How Data Storytelling brings stories to life.
description:
*Data Science Portugal presents*

**From analytics to narratives. How Data Storytelling brings stories to life.** 💡

Join us for the evening session of Open Lab Days #2, where **Salomé Esteves** will dive into the world of data storytelling!

Data is not a story, and not all data tells a story. But fascinating stories can sprout everywhere, if one knows how to pose the right questions. From raw numbers to meaningful visualizations, Data Storytelling is as much a work of creativity and spark as it is an iterative process and a skill everyone can work on. But how? Find out what steps you can take - and how to train them - to bring your data to life and connect with your audience.

**Agenda**
18h00: Get together
18h15: Talk: "**From analytics to narratives. How Data Storytelling brings stories to life."**, with **Salomé Esteves**
19h00: Drinks + Networking

**About the speaker**

**Salomé Esteves** is a visual and data journalist. However, because it doesn't come naturally for her to do just one thing, she is also a Professor of data visualization and infographics at the Lisbon School of Architecture. Salomé completed her PhD in Design at FA.ULisboa in 2023, where she researched Data Visualization within press journalism. Before that, she completed a master's degree in Communication Design at the same university and a bachelor's degree in Communication Sciences, specializing in Journalism, from the Faculty of Letters of the University of Porto.

Reserve your spot today and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

⚠️ Disclaimer: If you already signed up for our Open Lab Day at Pixelmatters [here](https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/events/307071524/?utm_medium=referral&utm_campaign=share-btn_savedevents_share_modal&utm_source=link) you already have a spot in the evening talk! **You should only register for one of the events. ⚠️**

title: DSPT Open Lab Days #2 – A Coworking Day @ Pixelmatters
description:

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to share insights, network, and spark new ideas!

More information on the invited talk soon **👀**

title: From PoCs to Production - Scaling LLMs with Quality
description:
⚠️ Disclaimer: If you already signed up for our Open Lab Day at DevScope [here](https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/events/306608191) you already have a spot in the lunch talk! **You should only register for one of the events. ⚠️**

*Data Science Portugal presents*

**From PoCs to Production \| Scaling LLMs with Quality** 💡

Feeling that mid-week slump? Take a break, pack your lunch and join us for an **inspiring lunch talk**! Join us on **March 19th** at **Devscope**'s office for "**From PoCs to Production \| Scaling LLMs with Quality**", presented by Nuno and Maria. They'll cover how businesses can efficiently transition generative AI initiatives from proof-of-concept to full production, highlighting key elements such as seamless integration, human oversight, model optimization, cost management, and centralized governance to achieve meaningful business outcomes.

**Agenda**
12h45: Get together
13h00: Lunch talk: **From PoCs to Production - Scaling LLMs with Quality**, with **Nuno da Rocha Borges** and **Maria Clara Jorge** @ **NOS SGPS**
13h30: Q&A
13h45: Coffee + Networking

**About the speakers**

**Nuno da Rocha Borges** is a Senior Manager at NOS, SGPS, where he is a core team member of SCAILE, NOS’s Artificial Intelligence program launched in 2025 as part of the organization’s digital transformation strategy. He has extensive experience in managing and implementing multiple machine learning and generative artificial intelligence products within the company, with a particular focus on enhancing customer satisfaction and maximizing operational efficiency.
He holds a Bachelor's degree (B.Sc.) in Economics and a Master's degree (M.Sc.) in Finance from the Faculty of Economics at the University of Porto, where he conducted academic research on investment decisions in R&D. He also has international experience, having studied at Universidad Carlos III (Spain) and, more recently, completed an MBA program at INSEAD, where he was awarded the Henry Ford II Prize. In the academic field, he is also a guest assistant professor at the Faculty of Economics at the University of Porto and NOVA SBE.

**Maria Clara Jorge** has been a Senior Product Owner & Project Manager at NOS since 2023, with the key role of driving the use of Artificial Intelligence to enhance customer experience and operational efficiency. Over the past five years, she has developed digital, agile, and strategic expertise, working closely with digital products such as apps and e-commerce platforms. In the last two years, her main focus has been on implementing Generative Artificial Intelligence technologies, from Proof of Concepts (PoCs) to production. She holds degrees in Communication Sciences and Management, as well as a master's in Economics and Innovation Management, all from the Faculty of Economics at the University of Porto.

Reserve your spot today, bring something to eat, and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

title: Complementary items recommendations using a RAG approach | DSPT & Porto Tech Hub
description:
Hello DSPT community!
Porto Tech Hub is celebrating 10 years! Join us on January 29th at Porto Innovation Hub, and lets celebrate together!

We'll have a talk by [Tiago Cabo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tiagocabo/), Data Scientist @ OLX group and [José Medeiros](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joseprsm/), Machine Learning Engineer @ OLX group about an use case of RAG for recommendations.
Don’t miss this opportunity to get to meet the community and deepen your knowledge on the topic.

Reserve your spot today, and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

18:30 - 18:45: Get together.
18:45 - 18:55: Welcome message.
19:00 - 19:40: Talk + Q&A: **“Complementary items recommendations using a RAG approach- an OLX use case”** by **[Tiago Cabo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tiagocabo/)**, DS @ OLX and **[José Medeiros](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joseprsm/)**, MLE @ OLX group
19:40 - 20:30: Networking.

=== Talk Abstract ===

* **Complementary items recommendations using a RAG approach- an Olx use case**

In this presentation, we will explore a cutting-edge approach to generating complementary recommendations for e-commerce items by leveraging large language models (LLMs) and semantic search. We will outline how LLMs are used to generate candidate recommendations and how semantic search enhances retrieval efficiency. A critical part of our process involved creating an offline dataset to evaluate and select the best embedding models for these tasks, incorporating domain-specific factors such as product categories and user preferences. Additionally, we introduce a reranking mechanism based on location proximity to optimize recommendations for localized contexts. Finally, we will discuss the challenges and strategies for productizing this pipeline, ensuring scalability and maintaining high performance in real-world scenarios.

=== About the speakers ===

* **Tiago Cabo DS@OLX group**

I graduated in Mechanical Engineering in 2017 and later transitioned into data science. My first significant professional experience was in novelty and anomaly detection, where I specialized in unsupervised learning techniques. I then shifted my focus to recommendation systems, working at Farfetch, where I developed personalized solutions for users. After two years, I joined OLX, where I continue to explore innovative approaches to recommendations, including the integration of LLM agents and advanced recommender systems. Outside of work, I enjoy outdoor activities, reading, and spending quality time with my baby girl.

* **José Medeiros MLE@OLX group**

I’m José, a Machine Learning Engineer specializing in MLOps. I’m a terrible data scientist and just an OK programmer, but I’ve gotten pretty good at making ML systems actually work… sometimes.I’m also doing a PhD in Psychology, focusing on consumer behavior, while also dabbling in painting to add some color to life.
When I’m not wrangling pipelines or pondering why people buy things they don’t need, you’ll find me outdoors with my kid, proving that work-life balance is achievable— at least until naptime ends.

title: MLOps in practice: Hands-on | DSPT #118 @Aveiro
description:
Hello DSPT community, what about a meetup in Aveiro? 🙌
Join us at Altice Aveiro for an energetic DSPT meetup. We will discuss MLOps in practice: hands-on with Pedro Sobral.
Check the event details below.
See you there!

=== SCHEDULE ===
• 18:30 - 19:15: Talk + Q&A: "**MLOps in practice: Hands-on**" by Pedro Sobral.
• 19:15 - 20:00: Networking/Coffee Break
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but might be an excellent networking opportunity! 🍕register [here](https://forms.gle/tgBNgsNQPkridcc49)

A special thanks to our venue Altice👏
See you there!

=== Talk Abstract ===
MLOps has become essential in today’s AI industry, ensuring the seamless deployment, monitoring, and scalability of machine learning models in production environments. With AI systems becoming increasingly complex, MLOps integrates data science and operational workflows, enabling organizations to automate processes, reduce errors, and enhance the efficiency of model management. Its adoption is crucial for keeping AI models agile, reliable, and continuously improving.
In this hands-on exercise, we will deploy three pre-trained models through a pipeline, triggered by a ChatOps mechanism (through github actions), that automates their availability via an API, demonstrating the real-world impact of MLOps in streamlining AI deployment.

=== About the speaker ===

* **Pedro Sobral, Master Informatics Engineering @ UA**

Pedro Sobral holds a Bachelor's degree in Informatics Engineering from the University of Aveiro and is finishing the Master's in the same area and University. He completed a research fellowship at the Instituto de Telecomunicações, where he started gaining experience deploying ML models. His master's thesis, developed at Bosch, is related to MLOps and Deep Learning models for sales forecast, reflecting his passion for merging AI with real-world applications, with a focus on optimizing ML workflows and developing accurate AI solutions reaching robust ML systems.

title: Product Management for AI and Data Products | DSPT #119 @ Porto
description:
Hello DSPT community! Join us on November 20th in Porto at UPTEC for an exciting event focused on the product side of Data and AI projects.

We'll have a roundtable discussion with [João Martins](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joaopdmartins/), [Jorge da Costa Ferreira](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jorgedcferreira/), and [Paula Brochado](https://www.linkedin.com/in/paulabrochado/), moderated by [Teresa Pessoa de Amorim](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tpamorim). Audience participation is encouraged, so bring your questions for the panel!

Don’t miss this opportunity to get to know more about, or get introduced to, the product side of Data and AI projects!

Reserve your spot today, and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

18:30 - 18:45: Get together.
18:45 - 18:55: Welcome message.
19:00 - 20:00: Roundtable: **“Product Management for AI and Data Products”**.
20:00 - 20:30: Networking.

=== About the speakers ===

* **[João Martins](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joaopdmartins/), Project Manager of Data Science @ JUMIA**

João Martins is a Project Manager of Data Science at the Jumia Group, holding a Master’s degree in Informatics Engineering from the University of Coimbra, with a specialization in Intelligent Systems. Before transitioning to industry, he conducted research on Verifiable Artificial Intelligence for the healthcare sector. In addition to his role as Project Manager, João collaborates both nationally and internationally to launch initiatives and resources that contribute to a better internet and is an active member of several groups within the United Nations Internet Governance Forum ecosystem.

* **[Jorge da Costa Ferreira](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jorgedcferreira/), Group Product Manager @ Maersk, Co-Founder @ ShopAI**

Jorge is a Group Product Manager at MAERSK, working on e-commerce business unit of the company. He began his career as a Data Scientist before transitioning to Product Management, where he became HUUB’s first Product Manager. At HUUB, Jorge built and scaled a high-performing product team alongside the board, developing a product and process that were pivotal to the company’s acquisition.
With experience across software and data science teams at both HUUB and MAERSK, Jorge has combined predictive modelling with software development to create seamless, data-driven software flows.
Beyond his primary role, Jorge founded ShopAI, an e-commerce company that for six years helped brands enhance shopping experiences through AI and image processing. He also teaches Python for Data Science at Gades Digital School.

* **[Paula Brochado](https://www.linkedin.com/in/paulabrochado/), VP Product @ Tonic**

Paula Brochado is a PhD in Astrophysics turned Product Manager sometime along the way. Formerly Head of Analytics at SonaeMC, she then joined FARFETCH leading a suite of ML/AI products—including a published multi-algorithm recommendation system, a robust search engine, a real-time personalisation service, and even a cheeky AI fashion assistant. Now, as VP of Product at Tonic App, she’s part of a team building medical grade, data-driven products for healthcare professionals. Beyond work, she mentors other product managers, sharing her love for data science-driven products and her belief that building them is two parts science, one part art.

* **[Teresa Pessoa de Amorim](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tpamorim), Senior Product Manager @ Critical Techworks**

Passionate by SaaS product management, with 5 years of hands-on experience across various stages of product development lifecycle. Master in Business Administration (University of Coimbra), with a specialisation in Strategy & Leadership. Initiated her international career as a consultant in e-commerce for brands such as Carrefour. With relevant professional experience across different industries. As part of the R&D team of the scale-up and unicorn Talkdesk, she contributed to the development of Artificial Intelligence features. Speaks fluently 4 languages (Portuguese, English, Dutch and French).

title: Advancing Healthcare with Data Science | DSPT #117 @ Porto
description:
Hello, DSPT community! We’re kicking off the second semester of 2024 with an exciting DSPT Meetup in Porto at UPTEC.

Join us as we explore the transformative impact of Machine Learning and AI in the healthcare field with insightful talks by [Raquel Belo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/raquel-belo/) from DevScope and [João Pedro Pedroso](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1298-7191), professor at the Faculty of Science, University of Porto. Don’t miss this opportunity to see cutting-edge applications of AI in healthcare and connect with fellow enthusiasts!

Book your spot today and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

* 18:15 - 18:30: Get together.
* 18:30 - 18:40: Welcome message.
* 18:40 - 19:20: Talk + Q&A: **“Machine Learning for Helicobacter Pylori Analysis and Diagnosis in Whole Slide Images”** by **Raquel Belo, Data Scientist @ DevScope.**
* 19:20 - 20:00 Talk + Q&A: **“Improving Kidney Exchange Programs”** by **João Pedro Pedroso, Associate Professor@ FCUP.**
* 20:00 - 20:30: Networking.
*

=== Talk Abstract ===

* **Machine Learning for Helicobacter Pylori Analysis and Diagnosis in Whole Slide Image**

The diagnosis of infection by the bacteria Helicobacter Pylori is often performed through manual analysis of histological images, which, due to the considerable size of these images, becomes an exhaustive task for pathologists. In this talk, we'll show you how we used Machine Learning techniques to automatically detect the presence of bacteria, indicating its location in the images. Additionally, by combining the detection of the bacterium with the identification of inflammation in the tissue, it is possible to perform the final diagnosis quickly and efficiently, also allowing visualization of the specific points in the image that support the diagnosis.

* **Improving Kidney Exchange Programs**

Renal diseases affect thousands of patients who, to survive, must incur in dialysis -- a costly treatment with many negative implications in their quality of life. As an alternative, patients may enter a waiting list for receiving a kidney from a deceased donor; however, waiting times are typically very long. For reducing the waiting time, another alternative in some countries is to find a healthy living donor -- usually, a relative of a person emotionally connected -- who volunteers to cede one of their kidneys. However, in some situations transplantation is not possible due to blood, or tissue-level incompatibility. In these cases, a donor-patient pair may enter a pool of pairs in the same situation, in the hope of finding compatibility in crossed transplants.
The problem has been studied under different perspectives, but the most commonly used objective is maximizing the number of patients in the pool for which a crossed transplant is possible.
We propose to change this objective by that of maximizing the cumulative patient survival times. This model departs from the previous deterministic setting, putting into play a method for predicting survival time based on historical data.

=== About the speakers ===

* **[Raquel Belo, Data Scientist @ DevScope](https://www.linkedin.com/in/raquel-belo/)**

Raquel is an alumna of FEUP. For the past three years, she has specialized in deep learning within the medical field, focusing primarily on research involving radiology and histology images. Her work has been recognized with publications and presentations at international conferences, establishing her as an active member in the research community, where she aspires to further her career as a data scientist at DevScope.

* **[João Pedro Pedroso, Associate Professor@ FCUP](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joao-pedro-pedroso-1202541/)**

J.P.Pedroso holds a PhD in Computational Mathematics (1996). He is a researcher at the Mathematics Center of Porto University (CMUP) and Associate Professor at the Faculty of Sciences, University of Porto, lecturing in Artificial Intelligence, Operations Research and related subjects. He participated in EU projects LISCOS, CIVITAS-ELAN, and FIT4U, on optimization models for planning. Also participated in FCT projects CORAL, mKEP, TEC4Growth, LastMile and Transformer 4.0, on tasks concerning optimization, simulation and machine learning, with a special focus on modeling and solving problems under uncertainty. He publishes and acts as a reviewer in leading international journals in the area, and co-authored two books on computer programming for optimization.

title: Exploring cutting-edge LLMs: theory and practice | DSPT #116 @ Porto
description:
Hello DSPT community we are back with another DSPT Meetup in Porto, this time at **[DevScope](https://www.linkedin.com/company/devscope/)**’s office!

Dive deep into the fascinating world of Large Language Models (**LLMs**) and Transformers in this session by **[Rafael Guedes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rafaelguedes97/)\, Senior ML Engineer @ Marley Spoon \| Data Scientist @ ZAAI**. It includes an interactive hands-on activity where you can explore one of these groundbreaking LLMs using the powerful **DSPy** library in Python. 🐍💻

Book your spot today and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

* 18:15 - 18:30: Get together
* 18:30 - 18:40: Welcome message
* 18:40 - 19:30: Talk + Q&A: **“LLMs: A Theoretical Overview of the Transformer Architecture and the Novel Concepts of LLaMA 3, Gemma, and Mixtral”** by **[Rafael Guedes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rafaelguedes97/)**
* 19:30 - 20:30: Networking.
* 20:30 onward: Optional Dinner

=== Talk Abstract ===

* **LLMs: A Theoretical Overview of the Transformer Architecture and the Novel Concepts of LLaMA 3, Gemma, and Mixtral**

This presentation focuses on providing the basic and advanced concepts of the backbone architecture of Large Language Models, theTransformer. Apart from that, it also compares and exposes the novel concepts that LLaMA 3, Mixtral and Gemma brought to the original Transformer architecture. It finishes with a hands-on activity where we explore one of these LLMs using the python library DSPy.

=== About the speaker ===

* **[Rafael Guedes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rafaelguedes97/),** Senior ML Engineer @ Marley Spoon \| Data Scientist @ ZAAI

Rafael is a FEUP alumni with 5 years of experience working in Data Science. Started his journey at Farfetch in 2019 solving time series forecasting problems and he is currently Senior Machine Learning Engineer at Marley Spoon working in several domains such as forecasting, recommender systems and marketing models. Lately, he has been writing AI articles focused on LLMs for ZAAI.

title: Unleashing Open-Source Potential: LLMs and Real-Time Data Analytics @ Porto
description:
Hello DSPT community we are back with another DSPT Meetup at Porto! This time at **[Critical Tech Work](https://www.criticaltechworks.com/)**’s office.

Join us for two cutting edge talks and the traditional networking session. The first talk is entitled **“LLMstudio: Powering Enterprise Grade LLM Applications With Open-Source”** and will be delivered by **[Cláudio Lemos](https://www.linkedin.com/in/~claudio/), Co-Founder of [TensorOps](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tensorops/), VP R&D.** To close it up we will have **[Tim Berglund](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tlberglund/), VP of DevRel @ [StarTree](https://www.linkedin.com/company/startreedata/)** showcasing the world of **“Real-Time Analytics With Apache Pinot”.**

Book your spot today and don’t forget to add the event to your calendar!

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

* 18:15 - 18:30: Get together;
* 18:30 - 18:40: Welcome message;
* 18:40 - 19:20: Talk + Q&A: **“LLMstudio: Powering Enterprise Grade LLM Applications With Open-Source”** by **Cláudio Lemos;**
* 19:20 - 20:00 Talk + Q&A: **“Real-Time Analytics With Apache Pinot”** by **Tim Berglund;**
* 20:00 - 20:30: Networking.

=== Talks Abstracts ===

* **LLMstudio: Powering Enterprise Grade LLM Applications With Open-Source**

TBA

* **Real-Time Analytics With Apache Pinot**

Analytics is arguably the oldest task of digital computers, and has remained an important part of business computing during its 80-year history. What could possibly be new in this space? A better way to serialize data? A better way to run distributed queries over a data lake? An exciting new integration technology? We need all these things urgently, but to be ready for the future, we something even more: we need analytics that can deliver results to users in real time, in the interaction layer, not to internal decision makers seconds or minutes later. We need what Apache Pinot delivers.
Pinot is a database optimized to serve analytical queries at extremely high levels of concurrency, at latencies measured in the tens of milliseconds, on data ingested from the streaming pipeline that underlies more and more of the systems we're building. It's an OLAP database, but one engineered from the ground up to power user-facing features, not executive-facing dashboards. In this talk, we'll look at how it's built, how to get data into it, how to query it, and the typical role it plays in a next-generation system exposing analytics-powered features to its users.

=== About the speakers ===

* **[Cláudio Lemos](https://www.linkedin.com/in/~claudio/), Co-Founder of [TensorOps](https://www.linkedin.com/company/tensorops/), VP R&D.**

Cláudio is a FEUP alumni. Two years ago, he co-founded TensorOps, helping clients accelerate their AI adoption. Currently, he is leading the development of LLMstudio, an open-source LLM gateway that helps users add observability to their LLM pipeline, while accessing multiple LLM vendors and optimizing costs.

* **[Tim Berglund](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tlberglund/), VP of DevRel @ [StarTree](https://www.linkedin.com/company/startreedata/)**

Tim is a teacher, author, and technology leader with StarTree, where he serves as the VP of Developer Relations. He is a regular speaker at conferences and a presence on YouTube explaining complex technology topics in an accessible way. He tweets as @tlberglund, blogs every few years at http://timberglund.com, and lives in Littleton, CO, USA. He has three grown children and two grandchildren, with a third on the way.
[https://startree.ai/](https://startree.ai/)

title: Hands-on with LlamaIndex: First Steps for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
description:
Hello DSPT community, what about a meetup in Aveiro? 🙌

Join us at the University of Aveiro (UA) for an energetic DSPT meetup, where we will discuss about Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with Guilherme Cardoso.
Check the event details below.

See you there!

=== SCHEDULE ===
• 18:30 - 19:15: Talk + Q&A: "Hands-on with LlamaIndex: First Steps for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)" by Guilherme Cardoso.
• 19:15 - 20:00: Networking/Coffee Break
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but might be an excellent networking opportunity! 🍕register [here](https://forms.gle/NinLy9Xz1eX6tXGTA).

A special thanks to AETTUA 👏

See you there!

**Talk Abstract:**

This session offers a practical introduction to LlamaIndex. We'll combine information retrieval with large language models (LLMs) to build powerful AI systems. We'll focus on the initial steps of setting up a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system, while also getting you familiar with core LlamaIndex concepts like Documents, Nodes, and VectorStoreIndex.

What you'll gain:

* Hands-on experience: Set up LlamaIndex, load your data, and build a search index.
* Core concepts: Understand Documents, Nodes, and VectorStoreIndex.
* RAG foundation: Grasp how retrieved information enhances LLMs in RAG tasks.

**Prerequisites:**

* Basic Python
* If you want to follow the session, bring your laptop with Python installed.

By the end, you'll have a strong base for building advanced RAG systems with LlamaIndex!

**About the speaker:**

* Guilherme Cardoso, Head of Data Science & AI @ Scotty AI

Guilherme Cardoso plays a key role in the growth of his team, focusing on optimizing and harmonizing processes between humans and chatbots for a more natural and intuitive experience. With a solid academic background acquired with a master's degree in Computer Engineering and Telematics from the University of Aveiro, he has participated in internal Data Science projects and H2020 R&D initiatives for Altice Labs and the Telecommunications Institute of the University of Aveiro. He is currently working on cutting-edge technologies for NLP (Natural Language Processing) problems such as Transformers, Entity Recognition, and Extraction, among others, and, more recently, on technologies based on LLMs (Large Language Models) - as is the case with ChatGPT.

title: Open data Initiatives. DSPT & DSSG Portugal @ Porto
description:
Hello DSPT community, we are joining forces with **[DSSG Portugal](https://www.dssg.pt/)** for this event around **Open data Initiatives!**

Ever wondered about building an open data project from the ground-up? The team from the **[Habitação Transparente](https://habitacao-transparente.dssg.pt/)** project is sharing with us their experience on doing exactly that!
Presentation by the team who brought this project into reality:

* **[Pedro Rocha](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pedro-rocha-632bb7a3)**, Software Engineer;
* **[Inês Xavier](https://www.linkedin.com/in/m-ines-xavier)**, Software Developer;
* **[Luísa Mesquita](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luisa-maria-mesquita)**, UX Researcher.

In the second half of the event there will be a **panel of three experts** in the field of open data presenting their insights:

* **[Filipa Castro](https://www.linkedin.com/in/filipafcastro/)**, Lead Team @ DSSG Portugal;
* **[João Bastos](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jo%C3%A3o-bastos-71458428/)**, Head of Systems Architecture and Data Analytics @ Porto Digital;
* **[Rui Barros](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ruimgbarros/)**, Data Journalist @ PÚBLICO.

Join us for this event at **[UPTEC](https://maps.app.goo.gl/cN81PbRFEYr4rcLF9)**

=== Schedule ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:15 - 18:30: Get together;
• 18:30 - 18:40: Welcome message;
• 18:40 - 19:10: Talk + Q&A: **“Habitação Transparente - an open data initiative for the housing crisis”** by **[Pedro Rocha](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pedro-rocha-632bb7a3), [Inês Xavier](https://www.linkedin.com/in/m-ines-xavier) & [Luísa Mesquita](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luisa-maria-mesquita).**
• 19:20 - 20:00 Round-table: **“The importance of open data”** by **[Filipa Castro](https://www.linkedin.com/in/filipafcastro/), [João Bastos](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jo%C3%A3o-bastos-71458428/), [Rui Barros](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ruimgbarros/).**
• 20:00 - 20:30: Networking.

=== Talks Abstracts ===
**[Habitação Transparente](https://habitacao-transparente.dssg.pt/) \- an open data initiative for the housing crisis**

Habitação Transparente is an open data initiative of Data Science for Social Good Portugal (DSSG PT). Our goal is to describe and understand the current housing crisis in Portugal through data. This initiative aims to collect data which is reliable, precise, relevant and contextualized, in order to promote a more informed discussion around such important topic. Anyone living in Portugal can contribute to and benefit from Habitação Transparente. Join us to learn more about our journey in building this initiative from scratch, as well as to discuss ideas and solutions to be built on top of this dataset.

title: DSPT#113: AI-Driven Approaches in the Energy Sector @ Porto
description:
Hello DSPT community, what about a meetup in **Porto**? Join us at **[UPTEC](https://maps.app.goo.gl/cN81PbRFEYr4rcLF9)** for a DSPT meetup full of energy! We will have two talks: “**Photovoltaics** **Asset Management: an AI approach**” by **[João Fontes Machado](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joao-miranda-machado/)**; and “**The revival of symbolic AI for sustainable energy systems**” by **[Ricardo Jorge Bessa](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rbessa/)**. More information below. See you there!

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:15 - 18:30: Get together;
• 18:30 - 19:10: Talk + Q&A: “**Photovoltaics Asset Management: an AI approach**” by **João Fontes Machado**;
• 19:10 - 19:50: Talk + Q&A: “**The revival of symbolic AI for sustainable energy systems**” by **Ricardo Jorge Bessa**;
• 19:50 - 21:00: Networking.

**Talks Abstracts:**

* **Photovoltaics Asset Management: an AI approach**

\- Scientific approach vs\. AI methodology
\- AI Techniques and their applications on PV asset management
\- Use case: ML\-powered macro\-scale autonomous battery management

* **The revival of symbolic AI for sustainable energy systems**

The energy sector is classified in the European Union AI Act as a high-risk sector. Therefore, aspects such as explainability and interpretability of AI-based decision systems are becoming fundamental requirements for AI adoption by industry. This Talk will present the evolving symbolic models’ concept and its potential to build real-time decision-making systems for human operators in control centers or market traders, and augment existing expert systems to improve existing tasks or perform new tasks by learning from data. These models are Inherently interpretable for humans and can be designed/trained in a human-in-the-loop approach.

**About the speakers:**
[João Fontes Machado, Senior Data Analytics Specialist @ EDP](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joao-miranda-machado/)

João is a Senior Data Analytics specialist from Porto, Portugal, with a background in Electrical Engineering. His experience spans across various fields, including Telecommunications and Renewable Energies - the latter being his most cherished, specially regarding Photovoltaics. With a focus on data, scalability, and automation, João excels at raising overall efficiency and scaling up systems. He is driven by the pursuit of distributed generation and self-consumption as the driving forces behind the Energy Transition.

* [Ricardo Jorge Bessa, Senior Researcher @ INESC TEC](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rbessa/)

Ricardo Bessa earned his Licenciado degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Porto (UP) in 2006, followed by an M.Sc. in Data Analysis and Decision Support Systems from UP in 2008. In 2013, he completed his Ph.D. in the Doctoral Program in Sustainable Energy Systems (MIT Portugal) at UP. He is the Coordinator of the Center for Power and Energy Systems at INESC TEC. His research interests include renewable energy forecasting, computational intelligence applied to energy systems, decision-making under risk, and smart grids. He worked on several international projects, such as the European Projects FP6 ANEMOS.plus, FP7 SuSTAINABLE, FP7 evolvDSO, Horizon 2020 UPGRID, Horizon 2020 InteGrid, H2020 Smart4RES and is the Coordinator of the AI4REALNET project. IEEE Senior Member, Associate Editor of the Journal of Modern Power Systems and Clean Energy, received the Energy Systems Integration Group (ESIG) Excellence Award in 2022.
[http://www.rbessa.com/](http://www.rbessa.com/)

title: DSPT#112: Consistency and Cost within XAI
description:
Welcome to another DSPT meetup at Aveiro! 🙌
=== SCHEDULE ===
• 18:30 - 19:15: Talk + Q&A: **"Consistency and Cost within XAI"** by **Julio Corona**
• 19:15 - 20:00: Networking/Coffee Break
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but might be an excellent networking opportunity! 🍕register [here](https://forms.gle/W15YZdYzLeSutHhv6).
A special thanks to our venue UA-DETI 👏

# See you there!

**Abstract:** As 5G transitions to 6G, there's a surge in AI's use for network advancement. However, the opaque nature of Machine Learning (ML) models has prompted a shift towards Explainable AI (XAI) techniques to enhance decision-making transparency and auditability. This study reveals the agreement levels of three methods—SHAP, LIME, and PI—across various ML models in 5G scenarios and exposes the associated time and energy costs.

***Bio:***
Julio Corona obtained his B.Sc. and M.Sc. in Computer Science at the Central University "Marta Abreu" of Las Villas, Cuba, in 2015 and 2020, respectively. Professionally, he worked at the University of Cienfuegos "Carlos Rafael Rodríguez", Cuba, from 2015 to 2022, serving as responsible for information security, application developer, and a full-time professor in subjects such as Artificial Intelligence, Data Mining, and Artificial Neural Networks. Since January 2022, he has been enrolled as a student in the PhD Program in Computer Science at MAP, conducting his research at the Instituto de Telecomunicações, Aveiro, Portugal. His current research interests encompass the costs and benefits of using Artificial Intelligence in 5G and beyond networks.

title: DSPT#111: Causal Discovery and Deep Learning (Braga)
description:
Hello DSPT community, and welcome to another meetup! Come join us at **[F3M Information Systems S.A.](https://maps.app.goo.gl/Yir3WFm4KYLLB8VYA)**, where we will explore **Causal Discovery and Deep Learning**, with **[Gonçalo Faria](https://www.linkedin.com/in/goncalorafaria/)**.

This Meetup will be held exclusively on site (no recording).
=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:15 - 18:30: Get together;
• 18:30 - 19:30: Talk + Q&A: **Causal Discovery and Deep Learning** by **[Gonçalo Faria](https://www.linkedin.com/in/goncalorafaria)**;
• 19:30 - 20:00: Networking/Coffee Break;
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ( );
See you there!

**Talk Abstract:**
[Gonçalo Faria](https://www.linkedin.com/in/goncalorafaria/) will argue that some of the hard open problems of Machine Learning and AI are intrinsically related to causality. Explaing fundamental concepts of causality and causal discovery to elaborate on the identifiability issues inherent to causal discovery with observational data. An approach to tackling these issues, in many circumstances, is considering latent interventions.

Gonçalo will present joint work with [André Martins](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andre-martins-31476745/) and [Mário Figueiredo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mario-figueiredo-138b099/), where they design a causal discovery method for this scenario using neural networks and [variational inference](https://www.cs.princeton.edu/courses/archive/fall11/cos597C/lectures/variational-inference-i.pdf).

**[Gonçalo Faria, AI Researcher @ Instituto de Telecomunicações](https://www.linkedin.com/in/goncalorafaria)**
**Bio:**
AI Researcher at [Instituto de Telecomunicações](https://www.linkedin.com/company/institutotelecomunicacoes/), working on the alignment and instruction tuning teams working on the [TowerLLM project](https://unbabel.com/announcing-tower-an-open-multilingual-llm-for-translation-related-tasks/). They aim to train a 220B parameter LLM that will be the best open multilingual LLM.

With a BSc degree in CS from Minho University, graduated with a master’s in Data Science and Engineering from IST. His dissertation, “[Differentiable Causal Discovery Under Latent Intervention](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.02336.pdf)”, which focused on causal discovery, received the [Luis Vidigal award](https://premio-vidigal.inesc.pt/pdf/GoncaloFariaMSc.pdf) by INESC. The dissertation originated a scientific paper with the same name, co-authored by his advisors [André Martins](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andre-martins-31476745/) and [Mário Figueiredo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mario-figueiredo-138b099/), presented at the 1st Conference on Causal Learning and Reasoning in California in April 2022. His main areas of research/interest are Causal Representation Learning, Causal Discovery, Generative Modeling, and Approximate Bayesian methods."

**Links:**
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2203.02336.pdf
https://www.goncalofaria.com/

title: DSPT#110: Unveiling Network Threats: Anomaly Detection in IDS - Workshop
description:
Welcome to another DSPT meetup at Aveiro! 🙌
=== SCHEDULE ===
• 18:30 - 19:15: Talk + Q&A: **"Unveiling Network Threats: Anomaly Detection in Intrusion Detection Systems"** by **Mário Antunes**
• 19:15 - 20:00: Networking/Coffee Break
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but might be an excellent networking opportunity! 🍕register [here](https://forms.gle/cvjdpsS35DW7ZFDe8).
A special thanks to our venue partner NEI 👏

**Abstract:** In this workshop, we will delve into the realm of anomaly detection, a cornerstone of Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), as we dissect an hour of real-time network traffic from the University of Aveiro. Through hands-on demonstration, we will uncover anomalies and identify malicious traffic flows, gaining valuable insights into safeguarding networks from evolving threat

***Bio:***
Mário Antunes holds the position of an Associate Professor at the University of Aveiro. In 2018, he triumphantly concluded his doctoral program in computer science at MAPi, where he garnered recognition as a finalist in three esteemed international competitions. His primary research interests encompass Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, particularly (but not exclusively) Distributed Learning, Federated Learning, tinyML, and XAI. His research endeavours have been effectively applied to Knowledge Extraction and Context Storage in IoT Scenarios, Evaluation and modelling of the costs of AI in NGN, efficient Multi-Knee detection for cache optimisation, and numerous other areas.

title: DSPT#109 | Pushing the Limits: From Kaggle and Experimentation to Production
description:
Gear up for the last DSPT Lisbon Meetup in 2023! 🚀 We are bringing you a mix of serious insights and laid-back vibes that you won't want to miss.

Join us as Tiago dos Santos unveils his perspective on the data science process through the lens of continuous iteration. 🧪📊

But wait, there's more! We've got Kaggle Master Konstantin Yakovlev in the house, ready to share his industry transfer hacks and maybe a few Kaggle insider stories. 🏆

And here's the cherry on top – we've got chilled beers and mouthwatering snacks to keep the good times rolling. 🍻🍿 Because let's face it, great ideas and great snacks go hand in hand.

So, mark your calendar, bring your curiosity, and let's make this meetup the talk of the town. Serious about data, but not too serious to have a good time – that's the DSPT way! See you there! 🎉✨

=== SCHEDULE ===

• 18:00h - 18:30h: Welcome and get together
• 18:30h - 19:00h: Production Data Science: From the lab into the wild by Tiago dos Santos
• 19:00h - 19:30h: Networking / Coffee Break & Group photo
• 19:30 - 20:00h: From Kaggle to Production by Konstantin Yakolev
• 20:00h: Closing
• 20:15h: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking! 🍕register [here](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSctfXfRrNwJEt1lrYQE6dok80icqNghiQl8G3J8C9vrLZH6gg/viewform).

A special thanks to our venue partner [Le Wagon](https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/lewagonportugal/) 👏

See you there!

\-\-\-

**Tiago dos Santos** \| Production Data Science: From the lab into the wild
Hey! I'm Tiago, and over the last 7 years I have been working in the data domain with different hats - such as Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer and Data Platform Engineer. I really enjoy pushing the value where it is needed and keep learning is part of it.

**Konstantin Yakovlev** \| From Kaggle to Production
DS/ML competitions reveal your limits and current knowledge. You have to push yourself one step further, expand your comfort zone, constantly learn and adapt. Participating in DS/ML competitions for more than five years now, and during this period in my life I've met incredible people - open-minded, passionate, intelligent. The DS community helped me a lot to evolve, and I'll be forever grateful for it."

title: DSPT#108 LLMs Unfolded: APIs, Semantic Search, Agents and Advanced Fine-Tuning
description:
Hello DSPT community, and welcome to another meetup! Come join us at **[Jumia Porto Tech Center](https://maps.app.goo.gl/pP7VQTSAm4jQGa1T6)**, where we will explore the amazing possibilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) with a hands-on approach by **[Luis Roque](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luisbrasroque/)**, title: **“LLMs Unfolded: APIs, Semantic Search, Agents and Advanced Fine-Tuning”**.

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:15 - 18:30: Get together;
• 18:30 - 19:15: Talk + Q&A: **“LLMs Unfolded: APIs, Semantic Search, Agents and Advanced Fine-Tuning”** by **[Luis Roque](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luisbrasroque/);**
• 19:15 - 20:00: Networking/Coffee Break;
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ( [https://bit.ly/DSPT108DINNER](https://bit.ly/DSPT108DINNER));
See you there!

**Talk Abstract:**
The "LLMs Unfolded: APIs, Semantic Search, Agents and Advanced Fine-Tuning" workshop offers a pragmatic guide through the advanced use of Large Language Models (LLMs). Participants will learn how to integrate LLMs with APIs using real-world applications such as a Spotify-powered music summarization tool. We’ll then move to applying FAISS and Sentence Transformers for building robust semantic search systems that can handle and search through extensive datasets.
The workshop intensifies with constructing AI agents capable of querying large-scale databases, combining tools like ChromaDB with OpenAI's ChatGPT for accurate, evidence-based responses. Finally, we’ll explore the advanced fine-tuning of LLMs with techniques like SFT and LoRA in a hands-on session fine-tuning a model for Python coding tasks.
This is a hands-on, practical workshop designed for immediate application, suitable for professionals looking to explore LLMs capabilities for innovative solutions.

**[Luis Roque, VP Data & AI @ Marley Spoon](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luisbrasroque/)**
**Bio:**
Luis Roque is a data and AI leader, researcher, and educator. He is the VP of Data & AI at Marley Spoon, enhancing business performance and customer experience through strategic data and AI initiatives. He is also an advisor at CableLabs, playing a part in the intersection of cutting-edge cable technologies and artificial intelligence, a Ph.D. researcher in Artificial Intelligence at the AI&CS lab at the University of Porto and the Coordinator of the Data Science Masters program at the Nuclio Digital School in Barcelona. He also co-founded and served as CEO for HUUB, a fast-growing startup that was acquired by Maersk.

[https://medium.com/@luisroque](https://medium.com/@luisroque)

description:
Welcome to another DSPT meetup at Aveiro! 🙌

===

**Abstract:** In this presentation, I dive deeper into the xOps buzzword explosion, with a particular focus on the AI/ML and Data side of these.
I explore the interplay between them, the pain points and the roles and activities usually associated with each I provide examples of challenges, available tools, and how these provide legitimacy to each of the xOps approaches
Finally, I provide insight into a H2020 research project where these challenges were felt, and approaches applied (RECLAIM Manufacturing - https://www.reclaim-project.eu/)

***Bio:***
I’m a Software Engineer, trainer (accredited), and team builder. I see my roles, both past and current, as a "transformation agent". I love to create environments for high performance on all fronts of an organization: People, Processes, and Tools. I exert a huge amount of effort to keep up with the technological landscape in a profoundly knowledgeable manner. Always up for a challenge.
Founder of Fiercely, DevOps Institute Training Partner, CKA, CKS, certified Jenkins Engineer, and a few more you can ask me about.

title: DSPT#105 Is this shirt a good fit? Drive AI into FIT fashion
description:
Welcome, welcome! In this DSPT meetup, at Porto (Auditório Pequeno @ DCC @ FCUP), we are going to do a deep dive in the fashion industry and online retail, demonstrating how a Data Science project can be used to create business value.

Join us at the [Department of Computer Science, Faculty of Sciences](https://www.dcc.fc.up.pt/site/), for the talk "Revolutionizing Garment Fit Assessment: A Data-Driven Solution to Improve Customer Satisfaction" and a networking session!

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30 - 19:15: Talk + Q&A: **"Revolutionizing Garment Fit Assessment: A Data-Driven Solution to Improve Customer Satisfaction"** by **[Miguel Arantes](https://www.linkedin.com/in/miguelarantes/)**
• 19:15 - 20:00: Networking/Coffee Break
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ( [https://bit.ly/DSPT105DINNER](https://bit.ly/DSPT105DINNER) )
See you there!

**Talk Abstract:**
In the world of fashion, achieving the perfect fit for garments has traditionally been a labour-intensive and subjective process. This session introduces our innovative project on fit improved through technology, which tackles the manual and imprecise nature of garment fitting. Our primary goal is to digitize and quantify garment fit, enabling accurate fit labelling for newly designed products.
The FIT project involves the creation of a robust dataset that encompasses diverse body shapes and sizes, in conjunction with detailed garment specifications. We then showcase our in-house clustering-type model designed to leverage this dataset to enhance garment fit. By harnessing cutting-edge technology and data-driven insights, our model is aimed at not only improving fit assessment accuracy but also reducing stock return rates for our customers. Through this session, we envision a future where the fashion industry can provide well-fitting garments to a wider audience, thereby enhancing customer satisfaction and minimizing waste in the fashion supply chain.

**[Miguel Arantes, Principal of Data Science](https://www.linkedin.com/in/miguelarantes/)**
**Bio:**
I’m Principal of Data Science at HBDC, I thrive on model implementation and, more importantly, love problem-solving. My journey from Licenciature to PhD at FCUP is a testament to my commitment to learning. You'll find me constantly on the move, shuttling between Metzingen and Porto, with intriguing layovers in Zurich and Amsterdam.

description:

*Bio:*

I'm the Head of AI Engineering at Sword Health. By fundamentally reinventing how patients access and receive care, we’re pioneering a more human, more clinically effective, and more scalable way to treat patients.

Currently I'm focused on growing the AI team at Sword Health and developing, deploying and iterating on ML systems that help to support and scale our mission of freeing 2 million people from pain.

I did my Master's degree in Biomedical Engineering at FEUP and completed my Master Thesis at INESC Tec.

Worked in different ML fields such as: NLP, Computer Vision, Time-series, Data Mining (Spatio-Temporal mining, Subgroup Discovery), Recommender Systems and Preference Learning.

title: Sustainable AI: AI Environmental Impact & Scalability
description:
DATA MAKERS FEST LAUNCH!!

The exponential growth of AI models raises an important question: What is the environmental cost of AI, and how can we sustainably scale its usage in the future? Currently, the computation cost of AI entails a substantial amount of energy, not only for processors but also for data centers' cooling, data storage, and data transfer. For instance, in Ireland, data centers account for up to 18% of the total energy consumption (Source: Central Statistics Office).
In this debate, we will dive into the current environmental impact of AI and explore possible strategies to ensure its sustainable development.

Join us to chart a path toward a future where AI technologies coexist responsibly with our planet.

\*
This meetup is organized by [DSPT](https://www.datascienceportugal.com/) and [Data Makers Fest](https://www.datamakersfest.com/) \- an emerging festival for all data makers \(www\.datamakersfest\.com\)\.

**EXTRA PERKS FOR PARTICIPANTS**

* Participation in a Giveaway to win a [Data Makers Fest (DMF)](https://www.datamakersfest.com/) ticket;
* **30% discount code** for all participants on DMF tickets bought until 11 August 2023.
*

**SPEAKER:**
[Luís Sarmento](https://www.linkedin.com/in/luissarmento/), Co-Founder and CEO at [Inductiva Research Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/inductiva-research-labs/)

**VENUE**
Kindly hosted by [UPTEC](https://uptec.up.pt/).

title: DSPT#103: "Machine Learning Platform thinking" with DevOps Aveiro
description:
For this year’s second meetup, we’re building a bridge. We’re teaming up with the team from DevOpsAveiro to bring together the two technical domains through two talks. Bosch Aveiro will be hosting us and supporting the community as they have in many different initiatives. Save the date and join us for the learning and networking opportunity.

The schedule for the webinar is the following:• 18:30 - 18:45: Opening the meeting
• 18:30 - 18:45: Opening the meeting
• 18:45 - 19:15: IaC utilizando Pulumi - José Santos
• 19:15 - 19:25: Networking/Coffee Break
• 19:25 - 19:55: Machine Learning Platform thinking - André Godinho
• 19:55: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ( [https://forms.gle/9XmvGE3dHKzzGNcf9](https://forms.gle/9XmvGE3dHKzzGNcf9))

See you there!

========================
**Title:** IaC utilizando Pulumi

**Abstract:** In the context of the migration of a project to a Cloud platform, we decided to use a new technology to manage the infrastructure, in this case, Pulumi.
In this presentation, we will show you a little about this tool and how it can help manage infrastructure as code in the cloud.

**Short-bio:**
José Santos graduated in Electronic Engineering and Telecommunications at the University of Aveiro and worked for seven years in the area of Intelligent Networks with several international clients. He joined Bosch in 2016 where he has worked since then as a DevOps Engineer in the IoT area.-

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-//\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

**Title:** Machine Learning Platform thinking

**Abstract:** The goal of industrial Machine Learning squads is to develop ML products and rapidly bring them into production to generate business value. However, it is challenging to automate, operationalize, and standardize ML products and thus many ML endeavors fail to deliver on their expectations - many times due to technical debt. In this talk, we discuss how can we can tackle this challenge by enabling ML Platforms that accelerate ML practitioners’ productivity through loosely-coupled shared services that also bridge the gap between Data Engineering and Data Science.Then, we will display specific services of this ML Platform and dive deep into leveraging Feature Stores and Data Mesh.

**Short-bio:**
André is an ML Engineer at DareData Engineering, currently focused on MLOps endeavors. In particular, directly building ML Platforms that empower Data Scientists, Data Engineers, and ML Engineers to manage the ML lifecycle from development to production.André holds an MSc in Data Science and Engineering from Instituto Superior Técnico.

title: DSPT#102 Visual Similarity with Deep Triplet Quantization Application to Fashion
description:
DSPT is returning to Porto, this time we're moving a little bit north to Maia, to visit the TecMaia.
Join us at this Meetup at Adidas Business Services where we’ll bring new topics related to Data Science. We’re going in "Visual Similarity with Deep Triplet Quantization: Application to the Fashion Industry" followed by an incredible Breakout Session where everyone can chat a little bit about their favourite topics
=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Talk + Q&A: "Visual Similarity with Deep Triplet Quantization: Application to the Fashion Industry"- Pedro Esmeriz
• 19:00-19:15: Networking/Coffee Break
• 19:15-20:00: Breakout Sessions
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ( http://bit.ly/3TbM1Yc )

See you there!
\*\*\*\*
**Abstract:** In this talk, we are going to approach the problem of large-scale image similarity with deep neural networks and how to deal with human semantics in the fashion e-commerce industry. The goal is “How can I provide similar products to the one currently being viewed?”. We will analyze the best solutions over the years, the main bottleneck at each stage, and a state-of-the-art solution that incorporates both triplet comparison and quantization processes in order to achieve an efficient, effective, and scalable similarity search.

\*\*Short Bio:\*\*Pedro Esmeriz is a data scientist specialised in computer vision and large-scale logistic operations optimization modeling. Over his career, he developed several predictions and prescriptive solutions that support digital platforms for several industries from e-commerce to supply chain and logistics. He has also co-founded an AI company focused on providing an end-to-end recommendations service to some of the biggest players in the fashion and sports industry. Currently, he is leading the data science team on Maersk E-commerce Logistics to address different forecasting challenges and how to optimize logistics operations. His academic background includes an MSc in Industrial Engineering & Management and an MSc in Data Analytics as well as having worked as an invited professor for the compute science bachelor at ISLA.

title: DSPT#101:AI in Medical Imaging-how to make models more robust and generalizable?
description:
The first 2023 DSPT Meetup is just around the corner!

Come and join us for our Meetup #️⃣ 101 in Lisbon. This time we will have [João Santinha](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jo%C3%A3o-santinha-0746b313/), researcher at the Digital Surgery Lab from the Breast Unit at Champalimaud Foundation, talking about *Artificial Intelligence in Medical Imaging - how to make models more robust and generalizable*?

**🗓️** 23rd February
📍[location](https://goo.gl/maps/QejCGAgMcseZmuYa8)

=== SCHEDULE ===
• 18:30 - 19:00: Talk + Q&A: Artificial Intelligence in Medical Imaging - how to make models more robust and generalizable? - João Santinha
• 19:00 - 19:15: Networking/Coffee Break
• 19:15 - 20:00: Breakout Sessions
• 20:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ([link](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1y8HJEo9WFRzqud0I0bHF6cNJx2aI8UsvLtD5qwj_OhM/edit))

\-\-\-

**Artificial Intelligence in Medical Imaging - how to make models more robust and generalizable?**

In this talk, we will learn about different areas where AI can be used in healthcare, specifically medical imaging. Then we will dive into specific applications of using AI to aid the diagnosis of oncological patients. Finally, several problems related to the robustness and generalizability of current AI methods will be explored, and solutions taken from domains like causal inference will be presented.

João Santinha is a Ph.D. candidate and an invited teaching assistant of the Deep Learning course at the Instituto Superior Técnico - University of Lisbon in Electrical and Computer Engineering. He is also a researcher at the Digital Surgery Lab from the Breast Unit at Champalimaud Foundation, where he develops AI algorithms for medical image analysis. His current research interests include medical image processing, ML and DL, imaging biomarkers, causal inference, and generalizability to Out-of-distribution data.

title: DSPT#100 Where did I put my keys? or an overview on Multiple Instance Learning
description:
DSPT is returning to Porto, and we are super excited to see you again! We prepared a wonderful meetup for you, great for newbies and seniors alike.
Join us at this Meetup at Porto Innovation Hub where we’ll bring new topics related to Data Science. We’re going in "Where did I put my keys? or an overview on Multiple Instance Learning" followed by an incredible Breakout Session where everyone can chat a little bit about their favourite topics
=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Talk + Q&A: "**Where did I put my keys? or an overview on Multiple Instance Learning**"- Simão Faria
• 19:00-19:15: Networking/Coffee Break
• 19:15-20:00: Breakout Sessions
• 20:00:Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ([ https://forms.gle/FzoCDpuDEyidMZpE7 ](https://forms.gle/FzoCDpuDEyidMZpE7))

See you there!
\*\*\*\*
**Abstract:** Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) is a form of weakly supervised learning based on the collective classification of sets of independent data samples. MIL methods were developed to deal with the cases where, in some tasks, labels don’t have a 1-to-1 relationship to each instance (sample) but to a whole bag of instances (set).
In this talk, the speaker will introduce the concept of MIL and how can its different methodologies be useful in your varied data science tasks. Usages of these approaches in health data will be explored through the work developed at iLoF.

**Short Bio:** Simão Faria is a Lead Data Scientist at iLoF facing the effort of leveraging Data Science knowledge to revolutionize health-related challenges. iLoF, with its innovative way of collecting optical signals from biological samples, aims to accelerate and support the development of personalized medicine. The speaker comes from a Biomedical Engineering background and has Data Science and Machine Learning experience both in research and industry. Some of his previous experience includes work in Computer Vision, Fintech and Image/Signal processing.

**Breakout sessions:**

title: DSPT#99 Learning from physiological signals
description:
Join us on this meetup at Altice Aveiro where we’ll bring new topics related to Data Science. We’re going in "Learning from physiological signals" followed by an incredible Breakout Session where everyone can chat a little bit about their favourite topics
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk + Q&A: "Learning from physiological signals"- Raquel Sebastião
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking/Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Breakout Sessions
• 20:50: Closing
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking ( [https://forms.gle/dxCoYMzhcHxvbCvn8](https://forms.gle/dxCoYMzhcHxvbCvn8) )

In case you need any support from DSPT feels free to contact the organization through the mobile 912305334. Thanks.
See you there!
\*\*\*\*

**Abstract:** The autonomic nervous system ANS regulates fundamental physiological states which are typically involuntary, upregulating and downregulating various functions within our body. The ANS controls
various organ systems inside the body, namely muscles, glands and organs within the body. While maintaining the equilibrium of the body’s systems according to both internal and external stimuli, many
physiological signals reflect the activity of the ANS. As it functions without conscious control, ANS reactions are difficult to deceive.
This talk will present examples of Autonomic Nervous System (ANS) correlates in psychophysiological states in different contexts, such as:

1. Human Assistance in Hazardous Scenarios
2. Support Wellbeing
3. Support Health
4. Biometric Recognition

**Short Bio:** Raquel Sebastião is a researcher at the Institute of Electronics and Informatics Engineering of Aveiro (IEETA), University of Aveiro. She is Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics, with a background in Applied
Mathematics and Computational Methods, from the University of Porto. She has been working in Human-Centered Technologies, pursuing approaches strongly inspired and oriented to Humans: from basic research to advance knowledge on humans, supporting people either
in daily activities or in high-risk events.

**Breakout sessions:**
Come and network with other Data Scientist enthusiasts, Don’t be shy! Bring your topics, bring your questions, bring your friends and network with us. We are going to have a few topics prepared but feel free to wander around and engage with peers.
