Talk 1: Creative Applications of Music Information Research
Abstract: In this talk I will provide a high-level overview of how music information retrieval researchers approach the analysis of musical audio signals, and in turn, how the results of this analysis can be applied for creative purposes. Given my research background in the extraction of rhythmic structure from music signals, I will place specific emphasis on the role of the beat as a fundamental component for music synchronisation which can enable remixing, rhythm transformation, and music mashup. Finally, I will reflect on the importance of the role of the end-user both when designing creative music systems and evaluating them.
Short Bio: Matthew Davies is a music information retrieval researcher with a background in digital signal processing. He obtained the PhD from the Centre for Digital Music at Queen Mary University of London in 2007. In 2011 he joined the Sound and Music Computing Group (SMC) at INESC TEC in Porto working with Fabien Gouyon. During 2013 he worked with Masataka Goto at the National Advanced Institute of Science and Technology in Tsukuba, Japan before returning to INESC TEC. Since 2014, Matthew has coordinated the SMC Group at INESC TEC and was awarded an Investigator FCT Development Grant in 2016. His main research interests include the analysis of rhythm in musical audio signals, evaluation methodology, creative music applications, and reproducible research.

Talk 2: Big (Unlabeled) Data: a quest to create a fish detection dataset
Abstract: Even though there are lots of saved image and video data available, most of this data is unlabeled. This means that the first step of a new Computer Vision project is the creation and labeling of a new dataset. This step requires lots of human hours to label uninteresting, boring, redundant images so that a (deep) learning algorithm can be trained. What if we could select only the most relevant and interesting examples from the entire unlabeled dataset? What if we could minimize the number of required labeled examples to achieve a desired performance? In this presentation we explore how we are using Active Learning to create a dataset to solve the problem of detecting fish in subsea videos.
Short Bio: Pedro Costa holds a MSc (2015) in Informatics and Computing Engineering at the Faculty of Engineering, University of Porto. Costa started working with INESC TEC in 2014 trying to find adverse drug reactions in biological data using ML. After a brief experience in the industry, Costa came back to INESC TEC to work on medical image processing using Deep Learning methods, having published in top medical imaging journals and conferences. He then spent 3 months working on weakly supervised deep learning methods at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU). Currently, he is Head of Research at Abyssal, working on how to improve Remotely Operated Vehicles’ operational efficiency using ML and Computer Vision techniques. He is also a researcher at INESC TEC and keeps cooperating with CMU.

Talk 1: Ernesto Pedrosa

Neural nets usage is all the rage, however, there are several challenges that arise once we apply it to a real-world problem, challenges like 1) making neural nets keep learning, 2) guarantee the quality of the training dataset, 3) Unbalanced training datasets, etc.
Everyone says that the big advantage is that by making use of AI/Neural nets their solutions will keep learning from user/customer interactions, but the truth is that few or no company complies with this commitment.
As training data sets are mostly supervised by humans, sometimes without a strong commitment to the project, classification errors happen quite frequently which end up damaging neural net accuracy.
Unbalanced that set pose a problem on neural nets learning. On almost every business certain subjects happen with a (much) higher frequency than others, therefore the training datasets become unbalanced between classes, which will make neural nets biased.

Short Bio:
Ernesto has more than 16 years of international technical, team leading and management experience in the mobile products and data services area.
In 2003, he joined to the startup MobiComp, acquired by Microsoft in 2008. At Microsoft, Ernesto led a mobile client development team helping deliver high profile global mobile products for millions of users.
In 2012, Ernesto joined CardMobili to take on the role of Head of Product Research and Development.
Mid-2017 he co-founded Automaise, being currently its CEO (& CTO). Automaise provides an AI+NLP based Automation Platform to deploy AI virtual employees capable of automating several business processes and tasks.

Talk 2: Pedro Delgado

Taking as an example the development of a sensor to identify the road condition, we will see the difficulties and technical solutions such as multiple linear regression, PCA regression, PLS regression and the elaboration of classifier models based on methods such as linear / quadratic discriminant analysis, PCA / PLS classifiers, SIMCA method and support vector machines. We will have an opportunity to see how these techniques helped to make decisions about choosing the best wavelengths to use to detect the different road conditions.

Short Bio:
Engineering degree in electronics and telecommunications and post-graduation in six sigma as black belt. Worked in telecommunications (PT Innovation) and automotive electronics (Bosch) in the development of new products and processes.
He was a coordinator on Bosch Car Multimedia innovation process. Presently works in the application of multivariate SPC methods based on PCA and PLS. As a member of simultaneous engineering teams, he emphasizes the importance of multidisciplinary skills, the ability to manipulate models based on first principles and data-intensive models.

title: DSPT#38 - Behind the Science: Data and Software (Lisboa)
description:
DS and ML are gaining traction, however, with great power comes great responsibility. The expectations of end applications and users are becoming more and more demanding. What can we do to keep up? Are we bringing out the full potential of available data? Saghir Bashir, from Ilustat, will discuss the advantages of having a full understanding of your data. What about performance? Can Deep Learning be a way to achieve breakthroughs? António Alegria from Outsystems will guide us through advancements made in the context of software development . Will one day software write itself?

Curious? Don’t miss this opportunity.

Talk 1: The "D" in Data Science

How essential is the data in your DS work? Do you, intentionally or unintentionally, give greater emphasis to which "black box" models to use and improving their performance compared to really understanding the data? I will use real life examples (including Google Flu Trends) to discuss the advantages of having a full understanding of your data, its origins and what it really represents, before you embark on any programming or analyses.

Saghir Bashir is the founder of ilustat, a data science consultancy and training company. After earning a PhD in "Biostatistics and Epidemiology" from Cambridge University (UK) he has worked on various real life data science projects in both the public and private sectors. The success of his extensive international experience is built on his pragmatic and collaborative nature. He is a natural communicator who actively engages his audiences in his presentations, courses and workshops. He is currently developing a series of "Getting Started" guides for the R programming language.

Talk 2: What if Software wrote itself?

Can machines learn to build their own software? Can they give developers more time to focus on value and creative tasks? Why is software still so hard to develop? Tools have improved but user and business needs have been increasing as well. We need to catch up! ML and, in particular, Deep Learning, have ushered a new era of Software 2.0 — systems that are more adaptable and evolve faster. Although this has successfully automated tasks in many fields, software development is still largely untouched. Why is it so hard? OutSystems AI was created to make software development a universal and purely creative endeavor, pushing forward in AI applied to software development. In this talk, you’ll learn about the progress of AI applied to software, as we dive into the latest methods. I will also share what the OutSystems AI group is doing to address current challenges.

António is leading the OutSystems AI group, an applied research team that is bringing the power of AI and ML to Software Development. Previously he spent 6 years at Feedzai, starting as the first hire in the Research team where he participated in the creation of their Fraud Detection and ML technology that underpins their flagship products. Throughout that time he worked at the intersection of Product, Engineering and Science to bring new products and innovations to market. He comes from an extensive Engineering and DS background, having spent the past 10 years designing and building disruptive data products in different domains.

Talk 1: Let Evolution Make you Fashionable
In today’s society everyone desires unique and fashionable products, which makes the ability to customise products almost mandatory in every online store. Despite of many stores allowing the users to personalize their products, they do not always do it in the most efficient and user-friendly manner. In order to have products that reflect the user’s design preferences, they have to go through a laborious process of picking the components that they want to customise. In this talk we will unveil a framework that uses the principles of Darwin's Natural Selection to create products that satisfies the customers individual needs.

Short Bio:
Nuno Lourenço is an Assistant Professor (non-Tenure) at the University of Coimbra, where he obtained is PhD in Information Science and Technology in 2016. He has been with the Centre for Informatics and Systems of University of Coimbra (CISUC) since 2009, as member of the Evolutionary and Complex Systems (ECOS) group. Formerly, he was appointed as a Senior Research Officer at the University of Essex in the United Kingdom. He is the creator of Structured Grammatical Evolution, a genotypic representation for Grammatical Evolution, and participated in the proposal of a novel approach to automatically design Artificial Neural Networks using Evolutionary Computation called DENSER.

Talk 2: Multivariate Statistical Process Control based on PCA applied to industrial processes with very large number of Variables
The process of printing and inspecting solder paste deposits in Printed Circuit Boards (PCB) involves a very large number of variables (more than 30000 can be found in 3D inspection of high density PCBs). State of the art Surface Mount Technology (SMT) production lines rely on 100 % inspection of all paste deposits for each PCB produced. Specification limits for Area, Height, Volume, Offset X and Offset Y have been defined based on detailed and consolidated studies. PCBs with paste deposits failing the defined criteria, are proposed to be rejected. The study of the variation of the rejected fraction over time, has shown that the process is not always stable and it would benefit from a statistical process control approach. Statistical process control for 30000 variables is not feasible with a univariate approach. On one side, it is not possible to pay attention to such a high number of Shewhart control charts. On the other side, the very rich information contained in the evolution of the correlation structure would be lost in the case of a univariate approach.

Short Bio-Pedro Delgado:
Engineering degree in electronics and telecommunications (Universidade de Coimbra, 1978) and post-graduation in six sigma as black belt (Instituto Politécnico de Leiria, 2005). Worked in telecommunications (PT Innovation) and automotive electronics (Bosch) in the development of new products and processes. Between 2011 and 2016 coordinated the Bosch Car Multimedia innovation process and participated in the settlement of partnership with Universidade do Minho. Presently works in the application of multivariate SPC methods based on PCA and PLS aiming an effective quality planning of new products and processes.

Talk 1: Customer retention, the machine learning strategy.

Abstract: Inovretail provides data science solutions and digital tools, for retail, that deliver quantifiable insights and actionable recommendations with direct and sustainable impact on key metrics.Our solutions are SaaS based, fast to deploy, easy to use and help retailers in three key areas:Stock Recalibration, Digital Sales Assistant and Multi-dimensional Forecasting.Within this context, Data Science plays a key role in the company: making the right questions and providing the best answers based on data. In this presentation, I will talk about one of our solutions to help the retailers. The Churn Analytics, our Data Science solution that returns a list of potential churners, enabling you to pro-actively develop churn mitigation strategies.

Short bio: Miguel Arantes holds a master in Mathematics Engineering from the University of Porto. He has been working in statistical models for over 8 years in credit institutions and IT companies. His priority is his family (2 loving kids), plays Pokemon GO, these days learning MongoDB, using the lintR library and also doing a few articles for fun in LinkedIn.

Talk 2: The Forefront of Fraud Fighting with Research @ Feedzai

Abstract: Prepare for a ride along the fraud-fighting pipeline and multiple research initiatives at Feedzai. We'll see a) how fraud starts and how transaction records can be used to identify databreaches and other points-of-compromise; b) how features based on historical records can be computed (actually, estimated!!) in very low latency environments; c) how we leverage Deep Learning to enhance the predictive performance of our product d) how our data scientists' work is facilitated with our AutoML efforts and; e) how we deal with the reject inference problem and avoid poisoning our training datasets.

Short bio: Miguel Araújo is a senior data scientist at Feedzai. Before joining the fraud-fighting cause, Miguel obtained his PhD in Computer Science at Carnegie Mellon University and the University Porto where he developed anomaly detection, pattern mining and prediction solutions in various domains using telecommunication records and social network graphs.

title: DSPT#35 - From Data Science to Data Life (Lisboa)
description:
And we are back again, this time in Lisbon for our meetup #35!
We will have two gents with us, sharing their vision from an experienced pathway over the Data Science field. Diogo Rendeiro from Feedzai and Pedro Martins from DXC will share their expertise with us!

Curious?!

The meetup #35 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.datascienceportugal.com) is going to take place on Wednesday, 4th July, 2018 around 18h30, at Hood, Edifício Altejo, Rua 3 da Matinha, Armazém 101, 1950-326 Lisboa

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: Diogo Rendeiro - Feedzai
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "Steering Data Projects to Success – Lessons from the Field" by Pedro Martins - DXC
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking

If you want to attend the dinner, please check-in here: https://doodle.com/poll/p64e4t5ri52usd5e

This meetup is supported by Bosch (https://www.bosch.pt/) and Feedzai (https://feedzai.com/).
Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

Talk 1: Data Never Lies

Abstract: Nowadays data is a prerequisite for any pitch to trial, ramp-up or sunset product features. Discussions on product and company strategy often informed by visual representations of A/B tests, KPI trends, NPS surveys, etc.
Yet we often fall victim to bias when presenting and consuming data visually.
In this talk, following the seminal work of Prof. Edward Tufte, I'll discuss common pitfalls of data visualization and discuss methods to spot and prevent distortions in how data is presented, and how to design visualizations that are effective and fit for purpose.

Short Bio: Diogo is an engineer by training, product guy at heart, and an early adopter of all things tech.

After a lifetime in suit & tie IT consulting and another wearing a startup hoodie in Berlin, he returned to Lisbon last year to work on AI products @ Feedzai that keep commerce safe.

Currently building data viz products that help anti-fraud analysts spot and understand fraud patterns.

Talk 2: Steering Data Projects to Success – Lessons from the Field

Abstract: Data projects come in different flavors, and require different skill sets. Whether you are working as a data analyst, data engineer or data scientist you will be facing a number of challenges that will create risks for your product, your research or your project. By looking at data from a holistic perspective, certain patterns and trends begin to emerge. Not only technical patterns but in fact human, social and organizational patterns. Pedro Martins invites you to discuss the key trends and patterns in managing data projects. Why do projects fail? Who are the key people in a project that will help you succeed? What is out there beyond pipelines, algorithms and AI?

Short Bio: Pedro has been working in the Data industry for the past 12 years. Always connected to Consulting, Pedro is involved in Data Warehousing, Business Intelligence and more recently Big Data and Analytics projects. Currently a Solution Architect at DXC Technology, Pedro has always tried to combine technical knowledge with business expertise.

See you there!

title: DSPT#34 - An AI a day, keeps the doctor and the spam away!
description:
Back at the Invicta city, the beautiful Oporto, this time we are gonna discuss two major areas where Data Science is making a difference: Health Sciences and Spam Detection.
The human body is full of mysteries and generates a ton of data but that doesn’t make medical data science more easier. Specially when medical personnel doesn't understand the data geeks...Kelwin Fernandes, from INESC TEC, is gonna show us how to correctly apply Data Science and Machine Learning in a medical environment and the hurdles to overcome along the way.
From human diseases to internet “diseases”. Duarte Coelho from E-goi has to deal with a lot of spam and spammers in his everyday work. How to use machine learning to get rid of the biggest annoyance of our internet lives? He’ll show us the way… Curious?

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Machine Learning in Medical Applications: from fundamental topics to practical aspects" by Kelwin Fernandes - INESC TEC.
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "Intelligent Analysis of Contact Databases’ Importation for Spam Prevention" by Duarte Coelho - E-goi
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking. Registration: https://doodle.com/poll/9qpbqr8ak7cgpdix
This meetup is supported by DevScope (https://www.devscope.net/) and Bosch (https://www.bosch.pt/).
Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

Talk 1: Machine Learning in Medical Applications: from fundamental topics to practical aspects

Abstract: Medical applications defy the most fundamental concepts in machine learning. Several challenges arise from the intrinsic nature of medical data: imbalanced distribution of the classes, partially observable data, data scarcity, need for model interpretability, among others. As ML practitioners we tend to maim applications to fit into our ML toolbox, building an unrecognizable monster that hardly resembles the physician's decision-making process. Using digital colposcopy as a test case, we will rethink traditional ways of modeling predictive tasks to overcome the difficulties imposed by this area. We will address topics such as ranking, transfer learning, and model interpretability.

Short bio: Kelwin Fernandes obtained a degree in Computer Engineering with summa cum laude distinction at Universidad Simón Bolívar in Caracas, Venezuela. He has helped dozens of companies to incorporate AI into their daily operations. Kelwin moved to Portugal in 2013 and started working as a researcher in Machine Learning and Computer Vision at INESC TEC. He is currently finishing his Ph.D. in Computer Science at Universidade do Porto on the application of machine intelligence to medical imaging.

Talk 2: Intelligent Analysis of Contact Databases’ Importation for Spam Prevention

Abstract: E-goi is a multi-channel marketing automation platform, with focus on e-mail campaigns. Spammers tend to try and use E-goi to make their job easier by planning spam campaigns. This talk will focus on how to ascertain if a contact database importation belongs to a spammer when it is made, through the use of a machine learning approach. We will pass through various relevant topics such as feature selection, implementation details, algorithm tuning through nested cross-validation and finally comparison between classifiers.

Short bio: Duarte Coelho is an average Portuguese 22 year old, currently concluding his masters’ thesis in Informatics Engineering on ISEP-P.PORTO, which is being done through an internship at E-goi. He obtained a bachelor’s degree in Informatics Engineering from ISEP. During his bachelor’s final project, he focused on the use of middle-ware technologies to coordinate actions in distributed systems at Xhockware.

title: DSPT#33 - Data Science your Life - From Your House to Your Car
description:
The summer meetup in Aveiro brings us a sneak peek into the work of major industry players in our world of data science. How are they trying to change our lives?

The meetup will be held at Bosch, Aveiro and they are kind to let us visit their factory (registration required on this link - https://goo.gl/forms/FugJ6ZchjuGSXTvW2). Due to time constraints, the visit has a limit of 40 inscriptions.

Bosch will bring us the challenge of dealing with the complexity of thermodynamics using big data. David Campos & Alexandre Chambel will speak to us about the challenges associated with house heating and producing cost-effective and reliable heating appliances. David holds a position as Team Leader of the Operational Data Analysis and Diagnostics Team at Bosch Thermotechnology, while Alexandre holds a position as Data Scientist.

But what about cars? Isabel Póvoa, Product Owner of the Vehicle Environmental Data Collection Product at BMW will talk to us about the challenges BMW faces moving into a driverless future.

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

* 18:00-19:00: Guided tour through Bosch (registration required: https://goo.gl/forms/FugJ6ZchjuGSXTvW2)
* 18:30-19:00: Meet and greet
* 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: “Generate value from operational data of heating appliances” By David Campos & Alexandre Chambel - Bosch, Aveiro
* 19:40-19:45: Group photo
* 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
* 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: “Preparing for a New Era of Mobility” by Isabel Póvoa - BMW, Munich
* 20:50-21:00: Closing, hanging out and some beers
* 21:00-00:00: Dinner (optional but the registration is required: https://doodle.com/poll/x2ggns29i7qgv4i4).

This meetup is supported by Bosch ( https://www.bosch.com/ ) and Feedzai ( https://feedzai.com/ ). Cheers to Bosch and Feedzai and thank you for your support!

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk 1:
David Campos & Alexandre Chambel
Title: Generate value from operational data of heating appliances

Abstract: This talk is about how heating appliances operational data can be used to create added value both for customers and company. It will focus on how to deal with operational time series and features, importing data to suitable structures for further exploration, pre-processing, visualization and insights extraction. The application of such processing pipeline is presented considering a use case where extracted data is used to understand general appliances behaviour. Finally, one can also expect a tour through the daily life of an analytics team at Bosch Thermotechnology and insights on how software development, agile management and data analytics are combined to achieve the best outcome.

Short Bio:
David Campos
Working at Bosch Thermotechnology in the Operational Data Analysis and Diagnostics Team, he is a versatile and enthusiastic individual that wants to learn and take on new challenges in the fields of software development, data mining, machine learning and leadership.

Alexandre Chambel
Data scientist at Bosch Thermotechnology (Aveiro, Portugal) mainly working on operational time series exploration, data analysis pipeline maintenance and development. Python, R and Linux are the election tools.

Talk 2:
Isabel Póvoa - BMW - Munich
Title: Preparing for a New Era of Mobility

Abstract: There is a fierce race for the first autonomous vehicle: everyone wants to make history. BMW is no different. In this race, big data plays a great role on the way to get there. In this presentation, I will share with you what I do and how my team and I are helping to prepare for the new driverless future.

Short Bio: Isabel is the Product Owner of the Vehicle Environmental Data Collection Product at BMW. Her main project is part of the backend for the autonomous vehicles. She has done her BSc in Telematics in UA and her MSc in Embedded Systems in TU Delft, The Netherlands. She has just published a paper on encryption and she is a so-called entrepreneur, pushing projects with Blockchain technologies forward. In her spare time, she is part of an association called female tech leaders that aims to bring more women to stem-leadership positions.

title: DSPT#32 - Data Viz - In and Out of Ourselves
description:
Once more in Coimbra, the city of knowledge, this time it’s our vision that commands the way. As Data Science deepens its influence in our world, visual information is becoming the queen. With João Duarte and Marco Simões, researchers at CIBIT-ICNAS, we will travel to the depths of our own brains and discover how can brain imaging and machine learning help shape the future of neurology and medicine in general. Will the machines become a sidekick doctor in the future?
From the inside to the outside, we will explore the importance of data visualization with Beatriz Malveiro, Data Visualization Engineer at Feedzai, who will take us on a journey on how our eyes consume visual information and how important is to shape good data visualizations for a successful data science project.

Curious? Ready to let your eyes lead the way?

This meetup is sponsored by Feedzai (https://www.feedzai.com/) and Novabase (http://www.novabase.pt/). Cheers and thank you for the support!

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

=== SCHEDULE ===

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner (optional but an excellent opportunity for networking). Register: https://doodle.com/poll/ut4a773kr5cbpvu7

Talk 1: Can brAIn imaging help Dr. House?
The technological advances and cooperation between brain imaging and machine learning communities have revolutionized modern medicine. Often, there are features in the structure-function relationships in both healthy and pathological brain that are invisible to human eye. The use of machine learning’s ability to unveil hidden patterns is increasing, especially in the development of new therapeutic strategies and diagnostic tools. This talk will cover different data processing approaches for extracting features from brain images (MRI and EEG) and their use to infer characteristics of the individuals and classify them into groups.

Short Bio:
João Duarte is a researcher in neuroscience at CIBIT-ICNAS, University of Coimbra. His background is Biomedical Engineering and he also holds a PhD in Health Sciences. He has been working in the area of neuroimaging and machine learning applied to both basic and translational neuroscience in the context of health and disease.
Marco Simões is a researcher in computational neuroscience at CIBIT-ICNAS and CISUC, University of Coimbra. His background is Computer Science and his work focus on the study of the autism spectrum disorder and the development of adequate innovative neurorehabilitation solutions, combining neuroimaging, machine learning and virtual reality.

Talk 2: Visual Explorations: the world of dataviz and its challenges in product
When it comes to data, looks matter. Our brains are wired to visually decode information so let’s understand how we can use it. In this talk, we will take a look at the world of data visualization and why it matters in the life of a data scientist: From the powerful and crucial role of visualization for data exploration, to the wonders of visual storytelling for effective communication. We’ll also explore how dataviz helps fight fraud at Feedzai and the challenges of building data visualization tools for product.

Short Bio:
Beatriz Malveiro is a mathematician who always took too long choosing fonts and color schemes for dull LaTeX documents. She has a MSc in Applied Mathematics and Computer Science from Instituto Superior Técnico and took on several graphic design projects throughout university. After graduating she merged both these interests and discovered the field of data visualization. Nowadays, Beatriz works as a Data Visualization Engineer in the Data Science team at Feedzai where she equips data scientists with dataviz tools to fight fraud.

title: DSPT#31 - Let's Talk about the Future with Data Science
description:
One more meetup in Lisbon, one more pair of awesome talks! Data Science has brought much change, let's talk about it.

André Melo, Head of Analytics & Insights at GroupM Portugal, will talk to us about the challenges that companies from the pre-data science world face when taking the leap into a data driven culture. The road is not easy but André will be our guide.

Talkdesk, in the voice of Daniel Ferrari and Manuela Almeida will in their turn talk about....talking. While leading the way to a new generation of Data Science powered call centers, they will give us a peak on the backstage of how AI will help call centers become more effective and helpful.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: " Information era: Forcing the quantum leap” by André Melo - GroupM
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "What the hell are they talking about?” by Daniel Ferrari and Manuela Almeida - Talkdesk
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking. Register: https://doodle.com/poll/ereqw3v227wdwqeq

This meetup is sponsored by Talkdesk (https://www.talkdesk.com/) and Novabase (http://www.novabase.pt/). Cheers and thank you for the support!

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

Talk 1: Information era: Forcing the quantum leap

Abstract : Most of today’s companies are not yet close to master the information era, specially those coming from the offline world. While broadly accepting the importance of data & analytics, in practical terms, it is no easy task linking analytical activities with valuable outcomes in a consistent way. Also, the knowledge production processes are highly inefficient, from the data extraction phase up to the final step of presenting the results. This talk aims to share some experience on how to tackle such difficulties and the evolving role of a data scientist as a major contributor for this to happen.

Short Bio : André Melo is head of Analytics & Insights in GroupM Portugal and an invited teacher for Data Mining in Nova IMS. His professional career has been related with information management and quantitative modelling in three main areas: Marketing - CRM and Mass Communication - plus Credit Scoring. He has worked in Banking (Millenniumbcp), analytics soft. development (SAS Institute) and in the Media agencies space (GroupM) while simultaneously teaching in Nova IMS for the last 10y. He graduated in Probability & Statistics(FCUL), followed by a post-graduation in finance & management (Univ. Católica) and finally a Master in statistics and information management (Nova IMS). Currently he is a PhD student in Nova IMS.

Talk 2: What the hell are they talking about?

Abstract : A call center is made of interactions: from calls made by sales teams trying to publicize and sell their products to customers calling for support and guidance. Are we able to automatically find the subjects (or topics) that appear in a call? Can we find the trends in topics over the last weeks? With the help of Latent Dirichlet Allocation algorithm, we will try to answer these questions.

Short Bio : Daniel Ferrari is a Data Scientist at Talkdesk. His background is in Computer Science and he also holds a PhD in Data Mining and Machine Learning. Daniel moved from Brazil to Portugal in 2016 to join Talkdesk and has been working in all-data-things-related: from data engineering to data science, passing through reporting and BI.
Manuela Almeida is a Data Scientist at Talkdesk. Her background is in Applied Mathematics and she also holds a Masters degree specialized in Numerical Analysis. She's been working in the Data Science field for the last 3 years and joined Talkdesk in 2017 to dig into its large amount of data and drive Talkdesk's data science products.

title: DSPT#30 - Everyday life AI: from the Interview Room to the Operating Room
description:
Who would have known that AI would broaden so much its areas of application in such a short time? With AI deepening their influences in our daily lives, let’s look at two examples in out next meetup.
Xing, in the voice of Katja Niemann will show us how Machine Learning and AI are becoming prevalent in our job searches and how can a smart computer help you land your dream job. Tiago Vinhoza, data scientist at São João Hospital, will show us how we can help patients and their doctors, by leveraging the knowledge of health records and from them learning patient outcomes.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:45-19:15: Welcome and get together
• 19:15-19:45: Talk 1: "Machine Learning for Job Recommendations" by Katja Niemann - XING
• 19:55-20:00: Group photo
• 20:00-20:30: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:30-21:00: Talk 2: "Deep Patient: Leveraging Electronic Health Records Data to Predict Patient Outcomes" by Tiago Vinhoza - Data Science department of Hospital São João
• 21:10: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:15: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking. Register: https://doodle.com/poll/8885cyufy4qk8mbi

The meetup is sponsored by Xing (https://www.xing.com/en) and Feedzai (https://feedzai.com/). Cheers to Xing and Feedzai and thank you for your support!

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-----------------------------------

Talk 1: Machine Learning for Job Recommendations

Abstract: XING is a social network that aims at enabling professionals grow. In this talk, we give some insights into the data engineering pipelines that we use at XING for building recommender systems.
We will focus on job recommendations and discuss challenges, architecture, features and algorithms that we use for recommending job ads to people and for understanding whether a person is actually willing to change jobs and an appropriate candidate for a given job. We will show how we put these models into production and what tech stack we use for building robust recommender services.

Short Bio : Katja’s main interest lies in making applications more beneficial and supportive for the users. She joined the XING SE in Hamburg as data scientist in March 2016 where she uses state-of-the-art machine learning approaches to better understand the users’ needs and improve the job recommendations amongst others. Before, she was working as a researcher at the Fraunhofer Institute for Applied Information Technology (FIT) where she focused on improving the learning experience for online learners and did her PhD at the RWTH Aachen in the area of data mining and recommender systems.

Talk 2: Deep Patient: Leveraging Electronic Health Records Data to Predict Patient Outcomes

Abstract: There is a lot of hype around using AI and Deep Learning in Healthcare. However, few real use cases exist. We will present how the Data Science department at São João Hospital is experimenting Machine Learning approaches to leverage its Big Data of electronic health records. We will focus on the challenges of transforming time-evolving heterogeneous data (vital signs, lab results, clinical notes, medication, and demographics) to create a universal deep patient representation and share preliminary results of ML models that predict adverse patient outcomes.

Short Bio - Tiago Vinhoza has a Ph.D. degree in electrical engineering from Pontifícia Universidade Católica do Rio de Janeiro (PUC-Rio). After moving to Portugal he was awarded a Ciência 2008 contract by the Portuguese Foundation for Science and Technology (FCT). He worked as a Research Fellow at Instituto de Telecomunicações and INESC-TEC on topics such as wireless networks, vehicular communications, information theory, and machine learning applied to biomedical data (heart sounds and breast cancer). Since 2017 he works at the Data Science department of Hospital São João.

title: DSPT#29 - Entering the freeway in a self-driving car towards the AI fashion show
description:
Back to the “Portuguese Venice”, the beautiful Aveiro, this time is AI guiding the way for another meetup.

Let's take a Ride on the ATLASCAR, the first self-driving car in Portugal. Our driver for this talk will be Professor Miguel Oliveira, Assistant Professor at the University of Aveiro. We will learn about what it takes to create a self-driving car and the challenges it poses to the team building it. Curious? So are we.

Jorge Ferreira and Hélder Russa from ShopAI will show us how to apply deep learning in shopping. ShopAI believes that AI can change the game in fashion and become your best image consultant. Is AI in the future of fashion? We'll see...

The meetup #29 of Data Science Portugal ( https://www.datascienceportugal.com ) is going to take place on Thursday, 26th of April, 2018 around 18h30, at PCI - Parque de Ciência e Inovação S.A., Via do Conhecimento, 3830-352 Ílhavo - Aveiro

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "The ATLAS Project at UA" by Miguel Oliveira - University of Aveiro
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "Looking for something fashion? Deep learning can help!" by Jorge Ferreira and Hélder Russa - ShopAI.
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking. If you want to go, fill the doodle - https://doodle.com/poll/a83qx4zhzksutb6f

This meetup is supported by Bosch ( https://www.bosch.com/ ) and Feedzai ( https://feedzai.com/ ). Cheers to Bosch and Feedzai and thank you for your support!

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk 1:

Miguel Oliveira - University of Aveiro

Title: The ATLAS Project at UA.

Abstract: This talk will present the ATLAS project. This project started in 2003 and has since that date been focused on the development of robots capable of driving autonomously. We will cover the design and development of small-scale robots that have participated in the Autonomous Driving Competition of the National Robotics Open. Then, we will present the ATLASCAR, the first full-scale vehicle driving autonomously in Portugal.

Short Bio: Miguel Oliveira received the Mechanical Engineering and M.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering degrees from the University of Aveiro, Portugal, in 2004 and 2007, where later in 2013 he obtained the Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering specialization in Robotics, on the topic of autonomous driving systems. Currently, he is an Assistant Professor at the Department of Mechanical Engineering, University of Aveiro, Portugal, where he works on visual object recognition in open-ended domains. His research interests include multi-modal sensor fusion, computer vision, and robotics.

Talk 2:

Jorge Ferreira and Hélder Russa - ShopAI

Title: Looking for something fashion? Deep learning can help!

Abstract: Deep learning is a very trendy topic nowadays, some call it overhyped. We will not contest that. Nevertheless, there are some use cases in which it can be extremely effective, even better than humans.

It performs high accuracy in image-based systems that can go from image segmentation to object detection and image retrieval.

Can you imagine a tool, built as a service, that allows you to walk on the street and picture the best outfits you find to later search it on the web? We will give you a glimpse of how to build it.

Short Bio:

Jorge Ferreira: With 23 years old Jorge completed a master's degree in Industrial Engineering and Management at the Faculty of Engineering of the University of Porto (https://sigarra.up.pt/feup/pt/web_page.inicial). He reveals a great enthusiasm about analytics and AI related with business applications, specially Logistics and Supply Chain Management. His dissertation thesis was focused on solving the problem of how to predict the volume of garments based on its attributes, relying on advanced machine learning algorithms and data preprocessing. Today he is working as a Business Data Analyst at HUUB (http://thehuub.co/)and attending to another master’s program in informatics engineering at ISEP. By night, Jorge has his own project. With 4 more partners, Jorge co-founded ShopAI (https://shopai.co/), a startup that is harnessing AI for building image-based search engines for fashion brands.

Hélder Russa: Hélder Filipe de Sousa Russa, born in Porto, Portugal and has 28 years old. He completed his graduation degree in Technologies and Information Systems in 2013 at Universidade Portucalense (https://www.upt.pt/) and then concluded the post-graduate studies in Information Systems at the same university. Finally, he finalized his Master degree on Data Analytics at Economics Faculty (https://sigarra.up.pt/fep/pt/web_page.inicial) in University of Porto with a dissertation thesis focused on deep learning on top of image segmentation of fashion items. With almost 5 years of professional experience, all his career choices always had as main goal to be involved in data management and manipulation, passing the last 3 years working as a Data Engineer in the Business Intelligence team at Jumia Mall (https://www.jumia.com.ng/). Here he had as main responsibilities the development and maintenance of the company’s data warehouse as well as the reports on top of analysis tools. Currently, works as a Senior BI and Data Engineer in HUUB (http://thehuub.co/)company having as the main responsibility to develop and maintain the company data warehouse. In parallel is following his dream on having its own startup with 4 more partners, mainly focus on artificial intelligence.

venue.name: PCI - Parque de Ciência e Inovação, S.A.

title: DSPT #28: The Bias Report: in search of equity in algorithmic decision making
description:
Back to the beautiful Porto this time we are going to change things a bit. From the other side of the big pond (aka Atlantic), Pedro Saleiro, a researcher at University of Chicago with plenty of Porto blood running in his veins will talk to us about a very important topic in Data Science nowadays, Bias.

Are we placing our own bias in our work as data scientists? What is the outcome of those bias in real life? How can we move towards unbiased data science?

Come, Pedro will show us the way….

The meetup #28 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.datascienceportugal.com) is going to take place on Tuesday, 24th April, 2018 around 18h30, at Natixis, Rua Santos Pousada, 220, 4000-478 Porto.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

18:30-19:10: Welcome and get together
19:10-19:50: Talk: "The Bias Report: in search of equity in algorithmic decision making" by Pedro Saleiro - Center for Data Science and Public Policy, University of Chicago.
20:00-20:05: Group photo
20:10-20:45: Networking / Coffee Break
20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking

Dinner registration: https://doodle.com/poll/4aerd9n5nixgkupu

This meetup is supported by DevScope (https://www.devscope.net/) and Natixis (https://www.natixis.com). Cheers to DevScope and Natixis and thank you for your support!

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-----------------------------------------------

Talk : Title: The Bias Report: in search of equity in algorithmic decision making

Abstract : Algorithmic decision making systems are being increasingly used in problems that can have drastic impact on people’s lives in policy areas such as criminal justice, education, public health, workforce development and social services. Recent work has raised concerns on the risk of unintended bias in these predictive models against people from specific groups, often under-represented, based on race, gender, religion or age, among other characteristics. We present an open source bias audit tool that machine learning developers and policymakers can use to understand different types of biases, and make informed decisions around developing and deploying algorithmic decision making systems.

Short Bio : Pedro Saleiro is a post-doctoral research fellow in the Center for Data Science and Public Policy, University of Chicago. He has been working with Rayid Ghani since November 2017 in bias and fairness issues in Machine Learning, mainly in the scope of Public Policy problems. From 2012 to 2017, Pedro was a postgraduate researcher in the Artificial Intelligence Lab (LIACC), University of Porto, pursuing a PhD (ML, IR and NLP) under supervision of Carlos Soares and Eduarda Mendes Rodrigues.

title: DSPT#27 - Minimize the Risk of Missing This Meetup - Automate!
description:
Psssstt…..we’re back in Braga, have you already automated your meetup RSVP to not miss even one? Are you sure you want to risk it?

This time Rui Quintino, a R&D Data Researcher at DevScope, will talk to us about Automated Machine Learning and how leaving some of the work to the robots might helps us in the long run. Will they become our overlords? Maybe Rui knows the answer…
João Lopes and Raul Azevedo, from WeDo Technologies will bring us a talk on the challenges that WeDo Technologies faces in Risk Management, where an open world that generates huge amounts of Data also exponentiates the potential of risky situations.

The meetup #27 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.datascienceportugal.com) is going to take place on Thursday, 17th April, 2018 around 18h30, at WeDo Technologies, Centro Empresarial de Braga, Lugar da Misericórdia - Ferreiros, Pavilhão C4 - 4705-319, Braga.

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Fifty shades of… Automated Machine Learning" by Rui Quintino - DevScope.
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "Real Time Prediction for an Active Risk Prevention" by João Lopes and Raul Azevedo - WeDo Technologies
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking

Apply for dinner: https://doodle.com/poll/dscdfmpzn4whtyfi

This meetup is supported by Bosch Portugal (http://www.bosch.pt/) and WeDo Technologies (http://www.wedotechnologies.com/). Cheers to Bosch and WeDo and thank you for your support!

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

----------

Talk 1: "Fifty shades of… Automated Machine Learning"

Abstract:

Is "the sexiest job of 21st century", the Data Scientist, about to be automated? How & when can AutoML tools help on a typical machine learning lifecycle? What AutoML challenges are still open & what ML work will remain in the foreseeable future? Most importantly… will robots get all the fun & sex appeal? :) Some questions we'll try to tackle on this session.

*-Robots are not allowed in this session

Short Bio:

Rui Quintino currently works on Data R&D team at DevScope, learning and evaluating data science frameworks & languages and how they can fit & bring added value to DevScope products & SaaS solutions offerings.
Beyond data technologies, Rui also has professional experience in web, mobile & security and he likes to mix all of these to make the world a better place through the use of data.

----------

Talk 2: "Real Time Prediction for an Active Risk Prevention"

Abstract:

Digital Transformation is reshaping the world we are living in, forcing businesses to adapt being the alternative a slow death as the transformation evolves. It is also changing the way Risk Management is done in order to overcome the challenges of handling the huge volumes of data continuously being produced in a more open and interactive digital context. At WeDo Technologies we believe Machine Learning is key to overcome these challenges and therefore have been changing the way we used to use it. In this session, we are happy to share the work we are doing in the area of predictive analytics and forecasting, based on one of the Labs Projects we have ongoing right now.

Short Bio:

João Lopes is AI Specialist at WeDo Technologies. He has a master's degree in Machine Learning and is an Artificial Intelligence researcher, practitioner, and developer with over 7 years in the industry. He has wrote a few papers and worked on popular ML software included in WeDO products

Raul Azevedo is Vice President for Product Development at WeDo Technologiewith a varied and rich background leading Information Systems in sectors such as manufacturing, technology, retail and banking. Raul is an AI enthusiast for long time, an area in which he specialized during his degree, having the opportunity to participate and lead several projects and initiatives along his career.

title: DSPT#26 - Real Life Data Science: Not an Easy Challenge!
description:
DSPT is back to the capital!!!!

Hey! Ever felt there's a huge gap between academia and industry in data science? That things that work in academic settings seem to stop working in a company environment?
In our return to Lisbon we will discuss why real life data science is not an easy challenge.

Jorge Ribeiro, Machine Learning Engineer at DefinedCrowd, will show us how challenging using crowdsourced data can be. Production and Academic environments can be very different and state of the art models do not always work as expected. We will learn how to overcome some of these challenges

Ricardo Domingos Ferreira, a Data Visualization Developer, will talk to us about data visualization, a key aspect of the Data Science process. A good data visualization can make or break a project and passing the right information to everyone involved is crucial, but how challenging is it to create those visualizations? We’ll see...

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "High-quality training data for AI with humans-in-the loop" by Jorge Ribeiro - Machine Learning Engineer at DefinedCrowd.
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "Data Visualization – a talk on Data Science storytelling" by Ricardo Domingos Ferreira - Data Visualisation Developer.
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking. If you want to go, fill the doodle -
https://doodle.com/poll/k3a2uewmph4gvtp5

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us at info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

------------------------
TALK 1

Title: High-quality training data for AI with humans-in-the loop

Abstract: Why is it so hard to reproduce in production environments most state of the art results you read in the literature? This talk will discuss what it takes to guarantee both high-quality and large quantity of training data in a short amount of time. We'll talk about crowdsourcing and machine learning techniques to predict the quality of labelled data, and how DefinedCrowd is fueling AI for world's leading tech corporations.

Short Bio: Jorge Ribeiro is a Machine Learning Engineer at DefinedCrowd. His work there is connected to one of DefinedCrowd's core values: achieving top quality human-in-the-loop training data at a large scale. With a background in Telecommunications Engineering and Computer Science, Jorge Ribeiro spent his MSc thesis studying how to automatically predict the quality of training data as people are labelling it.

------------------------
TALK 2

Title: Data Visualization – a talk on Data Science storytelling

Abstract: How can we take advantage of our visual cognition to find clear and elegant solutions to our Data Science problems in a daily basis? What is Data Visualization? During this talk we will explore the beginnings of it and find the right path to build nowadays Data Visualization environments. We will find solutions on which everyone can leverage and provide simple answers to the most demanding Data Science questions.

Short Bio: Ricardo Domingos Ferreira is a Data Visualization Developer, and his goal is to create both data intensive and visually insightful applications using modern software. He is also an invited professor at Porto Business School, for the Visual Data Mining course. He has a MSc in Biomedical Engineering from Instituto Superior Técnico, with a minor in Imaging, Biosignals and Biomedical instrumentation.

title: DSPT#25 – From Human-Machine Interaction to Human-Machine Cooperation
description:
Data Science and Big Data have brought new Human-Machine interactions to our world. Wanna discover how machines are entering the human world and helping solving complex problems? Come to our meetup on Tuesday, 27th March, at ISEC.

Nuno Lavado will talk about a project where subjects were recorded using CCTV cameras with the objective of early detection of abnormal behaviour. However, the sheer amount of data produced poses quite a challenge, both in the terms of big data and data science. Will this Human-Machine interaction help local law enforcement and health professionals detect problems before they escalate?

What's better than a computer and a great data scientist? How about making them work together? Bruno Antunes will show us how Novabase is harnessing the power of Cognitive Science allied with Computer Science to solve real world problems in creative ways using Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing and other techniques.

This meetup is supported by a 2018 Silver Partner, Novabase. Cheers to Novabase and thank you for the support!

Schedule:
18:30-19:00: Welcome
19:00-19:30: Video Analysis – Making Hot Spots Discoverable, Nuno Lavado - ISEC
19:40-19:45: Group photo
19:45-20:15: Coffee Break
20:15-20:45: Cognitive Computing at Novabase: Building the Solutions of Tomorrow, Bruno Antunes - Novabase
20:50: Closing
21:00: Dinner (optional) - https://doodle.com/poll/q7zqwif9aqvi2yze

Talk 1: Video Analysis – Making Hot Spots Discoverable
A work in progress project that aims to detect abnormal behaviour in video recordings and provide early warnings will be covered. Subjects’ were recorded in their usual environment using CCTV cameras during several days (3 TiB of videos comprising 528M frames). Feature engineering, based on unsupervised detection and tracking algorithms, will be discussed as well as its implementation using a task farming approach within the local High Performance Computing cluster (LaCED/IPC – 480 cores, 3.75 TiB RAM, distributed over 20 compute nodes connected by InfiniBand FDR). Features allow taking all the video’s frames through a process of content and relationship awareness between subjects.

Short Bio: Nuno Lavado is an Adjunct Professor at IPC/ISEC - Polytechnic Institute of Coimbra, Institute of Engineering. He worked at The Nielsen Company, based in Oxford, UK covering the Nordic Countries as a Data Science Business Leader. He has also been involved in knowledge transfer between Uni and Industry at Kantar’s Global Operations, Data Solutions Team, London, UK. He has a PhD in Quantitative Methods, Statistics and Data Analysis by the ISCTE-IUL Business School. His background also includes a degree in Mathematics and a MSc degree in Statistics and Information Management.

Talk 2: Cognitive Computing at Novabase: Building the Solutions of Tomorrow
The recently created Center for Cognitive Computing of Novabase, headquartered in Coimbra, is dedicated to applying the latest cognitive technologies, delivering the best solutions for the most challenging problems. We are a growing team of talented people that are passionate about what they do, never run away from a good problem and are committed to deliver with the highest quality. In this talk we will explain how we apply different Artificial Intelligence techniques, from Machine Learning to Natural Language Processing, to solve a variety of challenges in real world applications.

Short Bio: Bruno Antunes is the Head of Cognitive Computing at the Financial Services business unit of Novabase. He holds a PhD in Information Science from University of Coimbra, where he worked as a researcher in several projects in the field of Artificial Intelligence. He was co-founder and CTO at Wizdee, the startup behind a disruptive product that made business intelligence as easy as asking a question in natural language. He then joined Novabase, where he leads a team dedicated to applying the latest cognitive technologies to the most complex problems.

title: DSPT#24- Let’s DREAM together solving challenges on cancer research and Big Data
description:
Ready to be challenged? We invite you to come to our next meetup in Braga! Two very interesting and challenging topics in Data Science will be presented.

The "DREAM Challenges", crowdsourcing challenges examining problems in biology and medicine, pose fundamental questions that can change our world. Miguel Rocha (Bioinformatics Master Director in University of Minho) will talk about his experience working in these challenges on cancer research.
But the challenges do not only stay within biology...how about big data in an enterprise setting ? The scalability and flexibility of data solutions pose quite a challenge and we will have Carlos Costa (Senior Big Data Engineer at Centre for Computer Graphics) showing us the state of art architectures and techniques used on big data world.

The meetup #24 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.datascienceportugal.com) is going to take place on Thursday, 22th March, 2018 around 18h30, at Startup Braga, Praça Conde de Agrolongo 123, 4700-314 Braga.

This meetup is supported by DevScope (https://www.devscope.net/), a 2018 Silver Sponsor. Cheers to DevScope and thank you for your support!

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Big Data Warehouses: Solving Big Data Challenges with Innovative Techniques and Technologies" by Carlos Costa - Centre for Computer Graphics.
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "Machine learning for cancer research: experiences in the Dream challenges" by Miguel Rocha - University of Minho
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

Apply for dinner: https://doodle.com/poll/iz5sd9e7sxdtnam4

See you there!

----------

Talk 1: "Big Data Warehouses: Solving Big Data Challenges with Innovative Techniques and Technologies" by Carlos Costa - Centre for Computer Graphics

Abstract:
We currently live in a world drowning in huge volumes of data coming from different sources at increasing velocities. This phenomenon is frequently mentioned as Big Data. The Big Data concept is far from consensual, but one can define it "as data that is too big, too fast or hard for existing tools to process" [1], either in transactional or analytical contexts. In this talk, we will be focusing on Big Data Analytics applications supported by a Big Data Warehouse, i.e., a Data Warehouse on steroids: high performance through distributed processing and insane scalability; flexible (but still structured) storage and data modeling; use of commodity hardware and open source technology to lower the costs of implementation; high frequency data ingestion; and mixed and complex analytical workloads (e.g., ad hoc querying, inclusion of results from unstructured or structured Data Mining/Machine Learning, highly intensive geospatial visualizations). We will be discussing several state-of-the art techniques and technologies, from the architectural point of view to the implementation details through open source projects (Apache Hadoop, Apache Hive, Apache Spark, Apache Kafka, Apache Cassandra and Presto).

1. Madden, S.: From databases to big data. IEEE Internet Computing. 16, 4-6 (2012). doi:http://dx.doi.org/10.1109/MIC.2012.50

Short Bio:
Carlos Costa obtained his Master's degree in Engineering and Management of Information Systems in 2015 at the University of Minho. He is currently a Senior Big Data Engineer at Centre for Computer Graphics (CCG), and an Invited Lecturer and PhD student in Information Systems at the University of Minho. Some of his previous professional experience includes Research & Development in the SusCity MIT Portugal project and software development & design. He is an affictionato of Big Data open source technology, mainly Apache Hadoop, Apache Hive, Apache Spark, Apache Kafka, Apache Cassandra and Presto. Besides professional and academic experience in Big Data, his journey is also based on successfully participating in several courses, workshops, contexts, and awards. He is an active researcher in this area, and has contributed with several peer-reviewed publications mostly focused on efficient architectural patterns and models for Big Data Warehousing, applied to general or specific contexts (e.g., Smart Cities, Industry 4.0). Besides his contributes to the scientific community, he also actively pursues the development of open source initiatives, because he feels that the open source movement was the foundation of the Big Data paradigm shift and must continue to be.

----------

Talk 2: "Machine learning for cancer research: experiences in the Dream challenges" by Miguel Rocha - University of Minho

Abstract:

In this talk, we will discuss some of the approaches followed in our participation in a few Dream challenges. These are community-based competitions organized to address the development of solutions for fundamental questions mainly in translational medicine, fostering collaborations among the teams involved.
We have participated in a few competitions, all related to cancer research, developing machine learning pipelines that could attain predictive models based on the data made available to the participants. The challenges included tasks such as predicting gene essentiality, predicting overall survival of patients with metastatic cancer, assessing sensitivity of cell lines to drug combinations or stratifying patients of multiple myeloma based on risk profiles. Available data for these challenges included clinical data on the patients, gene expression data, chemical structures of drugs/compounds and related features, among others.

Short Bio:

Miguel Rocha is an Associate Professor at the Dept. Informatics in the University of Minho, where he is also a senior researcher at the Centre of Biological Engineering, and the Director of the Master in Bioinformatics. His main research interests lie in the fields of Bioinformatics, Systems Biology and Data Mining, areas where he applies methods such as Evolutionary Algorithms, Neural Networks and other Machine Learning models to problems in biological data analysis, metabolic engineering and the optimization of computer networks

title: DSPT#23 - Data Science and AI in production-critical environments
description:
Hey everyone!
Time for another meetup in Porto. Let's meet to discuss Data Science and complain about the bad weather while having some beers :)

This time we will have two new speakers talking about deploying AI systems in production-ready and critical environments.

Luís Matias is coming all the way from NEC Laboratories Europe (Heidelberg, Germany) to speak about the challenges of deploying AI-based software in domains like transportation and logistics.

We will also have Tiago Baptista from Critical Software presenting how they deploy AI and Machine Learning systems in critical projects. The company provides systems and software services for safety, mission and business-critical applications ranging from aerospace, defense and security, government, finance and many others domains.

The 23rd meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Thursday, 8th March, 2018 around 18h30, at UPTEC - main auditorium - Allen 455, R. Alfredo Allen, Porto.

This meetup will be sponsored by Critical Software.

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: “Data Science NOW! (and other not so deep thoughts about AI)”, presented by Luis Matias , NEC Laboratories Europe (Heidelberg, Germany)
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break.
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: AI&ML @Critical Software: A Guided Tour, presented by Tiago Baptista , Critical Software
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking. You can register on the following list.

Dinner
https://doodle.com/poll/5rkew4nc43zrxqp8

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!
-------------------------------------

Talk 1: “Data Science NOW! (and other not so deep thoughts about AI)”, Luis Matias from NEC Laboratories Europe (Heidelberg, Germany)

In this presentation, we approach the issue of defining Data Science as the ability to transform raw data into information in an (almost…) automated fashion. We highlight the different stages that may precede the deployment of AI-systems in production-ready environments that actually enclose a good value proposition by using concrete examples of accomplished projects from Transport and Logistics (with open discussion for other verticals). We will also highlight the power of underestimated DS stages nowadays such as data wrangling, exploratory data analysis, visualization and interpretability on providing insights on how valuable your data and models are for your business. I hope to minimize the amount of throws of rotten tomatoes by making the audience laugh now and then with not so deep stuff.

Short Bio: Dr. Luis Moreira-Matias received his Ms.c. degree in Informatics Engineering and Ph.d. degree in Computer Science (major in Machine Learning) from the University of Porto, in 2009 and 2015, respectively. Luis served in the Program Committee and/or as invited reviewer of multiple high-impact research venues such as KDD, AAAI, IEEE TKDE, ESWA, ECML/PKDD and KAIS, among others. Moreover, he encloses a successful track record of real-world deployment of AI-based software products across EMEA and APAC. Currently, he is Senior Researcher at NEC Laboratories Europe (Heidelberg, Germany), where he is fortunate to lead R&D of AI-based software for Transport, Retail and Fintech industries. He was fortunate to be invited to give keynotes around the globe, ranging locations from Brisbane (Australia) to Las Palmas (Spain). Academically, Luis authored 40+ high-impact peer-reviewer publications on related topics.

Talk 2: AI&ML @Critical Software: A Guided Tour, Tiago Baptista , Critical Software

This talk will give a general overview of the Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning area at Critical Software. Within the company, the department has formally started in mid 2017 as an effort to organize internal knowledge and provide new AI focused products and services. We will give an overview of how this area is seen at Critical and provide a general description of some our projects in AI&ML, and, whenever possible, discuss some of the approaches used.

Short Bio: Tiago Baptista is a Senior Engineer at Critical Software, working as a member of Artificial Intelligence team. He holds a B.Sc. and a Ph.D., both in Informatics Engineering from that same university. He is a member of the Evolutionary and Complex Systems research group (ECOS) of the Centre for Informatics and Systems of the University of Coimbra (CISUC), where he conducts research mostly on evolutionary computation, artificial life, and complex systems. Previously, he was an Assistant Professor at the Department of Informatics Engineering of the University of Coimbra, where he taught courses such as Introduction to Artificial Intelligence, Introduction to Programming and Problem Solving, or Complex Systems. During his Ph.D. work he created a software multi-agent framework, called BitBang, focused on the simulation of complex systems and artificial life. He authored several peer-reviewed articles published in international journals and conference proceedings.

title: DSPT#22 - Welcome, smart people, to the smart city
description:
Hey everyone! It's time for another meetup and this time we're back in Aveiro. The portuguese Venice, as they say, is undoubtedly a beautiful city. But is it smart? What even is a smart city? And what role does Data Science play in making a city smarter? We’ll talk about this, and much more, in another inspiring Data Science Portugal event. Come for the answers, stay for the beer :)

The meetup #22 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Tuesday, 20th February, 2018 around 18h30, at IEUA - Incubadora de Empresas da Universidade de Aveiro, Campus Universitário de Santiago, Edifício 1, 3810 - 193 Aveiro.

=== SCHEDULE ===
The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Smart Cities and Data Science" by João Garcia - Ubiwhere.
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break.
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: Rota (RObô Triciclo para condução Autónoma) by Artur Pereira
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking. You can register on the following list https://doodle.com/poll/pvk9rehisrmku8nh

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!
-------------------------------------

Talk 1: "Smart Cities and Data Science at Ubiwhere"
Smart Cities is an emerging and exciting area with data at its core. Generated through different means (sensors, crowdsourcing) and from different domains (parking, waste management, air quality, traffic, etc), the large quantities of data generated by a fully fledged smart city can present interesting challenges. Be it from an interoperability point of view, a question of being able to handle large quantities of data or simply handling the problem of analysing or making predictions upon highly irregular data, when working on this field, you will be constantly presented with interesting challenges.

Short Bio:
João Garcia is a Data Scientist and general Software Engineer at Ubiwhere. He holds an MSc. Degree in Computer Science from the University of Coimbra. At Ubiwhere, he designed and implemented a recommendation system for the real-estate market in Lisbon. He also has been involved in Citibrain Smart Cities platform, making predictions on time-series data regarding parking availability. Participated in other projects, such as Águas do Porto, where he has developed models to generate water leakages’ alerts and heavily involved in the European project symbIoTe, where he contributes to the design and implementation of the overall system, as well as for Ubiwhere’s Use Case, Smart Mobility and Ecological Urban Routing.

Talk 2: "Rota (RObô Triciclo para condução Autónoma)"
This talk will focus on the ROTA project, a teaching and research project of the Department of Electronics, Telecommunications and Informatics of the University of Aveiro. Initially, a description of the event and its challenges will be made. Next, the ROTA project vehicle will be presented, including the decisions made in its design and implementation. Finally, we will be able to present and discuss some of the challenges of the field of robotics that such a test involves, such as perception, location, mapping, trajectory planning and vehicle movement control.

Short Bio:
Artur Pereira was born in Vila Nova de Famalicão, Portugal, in April of 1960. He graduated in Electronic Engineering and Telecommunications at the University of Aveiro in 1984. He received his PhD in Electrotechnical Engineering from the University of Aveiro in 2003.
Currently, he is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electronics, Telecommunications and Informatics at the University of Aveiro and Researcher at the Laboratory of Robotics and Intelligent Systems (IRIS Lab) of the Institute of Electronic and Computer Engineering of Aveiro (IEETA).
