title: DSPT#21 - Welcome to the Meetup of the Year... Deep Learning and Big Data
description:
Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the event of this year!! As Data Science Portugal meetups return for another year full of events and surprises in the A.I. field, we start by bringing you live to one of the hottest debates: what is Tensorflow, and is it really Machine Learning?! Carlos Soares from University of Porto demonstrates why TensorFlow IS NOT machine learning and why Deep Learning might not be the best answer for every problem. Ricardo Teixeira from Talkdesk will share with us an overview of data lake architectures, their benefits, drawbacks and their usefulness to data scientists.

Wanna start this New DS Year on the right foot? This is it! Join us for another inspiring event at Porto and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™! By the way... we have a fresh stock of B.E.E.R at your disposal 😉

The meetup #21 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Wednesday, 24th January, 2018 around 18h30, at UPTEC - main auditorium - Allen 455, R. Alfredo Allen, 4000 Porto.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Data Lake architectures and Data Science: Diving in." by Ricardo Teixeira - Talkdesk.
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break.
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "TensorFlow is NOT machine learning" by Carlos Soares - University of Porto.
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!
-------------------------------------

Talk1: "Data Lake architectures and Data Science: Diving in." by Ricardo Teixeira

Abstract:
Let's get our feet wet in data lake architectures, understand their benefits, drawbacks and their usefulness to data scientists.

Short Bio:
Ricardo is a Senior Data Engineer at Talkdesk with the role of tech lead for the Data Engineering team and involved in the evolution of Talkdesk's data platform. Before, he was the lead engineer for Farfech's Big Data team.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: "TensorFlow is NOT machine learning" by Carlos Soares

Abstract:
Machine Learning/Data Mining/Data Science/Statistics/{your favorite name here} are reasonably complex fields, which require an understanding of several basic concepts that can only be obtained with a solid education. The combination of the DeepLearning hype with the availability of tools, such as TensorFlow, enabling us to obtain results quickly has lead to the illusion that those basic concepts are not necessary in order to become a good data scientist. On the other hand, I might just be jealous of those young people doing cool stuff with Deep Learning. We’ll discuss these issues during this talk.

Short Bio:
Carlos Soares received an undergraduate degree (licenciatura) in Systems Engineering and Informatics from Universidade do Minho, Portugal, an M.Sc. degree in Artificial Intelligence and a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Universidade do Porto, Portugal. After 15 years at the Faculty of Economics, he is now an Associate Professor at the Faculty of Engineering of Universidade do Porto. Carlos also teaches at PBS (Porto Business School, formerly EGP-UPBS) and he is a post-graduation supervisor at the Universidade de São Paulo. Carlos is a researcher at INESC TEC, focusing on metalearning/automML but with a general interest in Machine Learning/Data Mining, Business Intelligence and Evolutionary Computation. He has participated in more than 20 national and international R&ID as well as consulting projects. His collaborations with companies include SMARKIO, Farfetch, InovRetail, WeDo, CreativeSystems, Bivolino, among others. Carlos has published/edited several books and more than 100 papers in journals and conferences, of which more than 50 are indexed by ISI. He has participated in the organization of several events, including KDD 2009 and ECML PKDD 2012 and he was one of the programme chairs for ECML PKDD 2015 and IDA 2016. He is currently a member of the Executive Committee of the Department of Informatics Engineering. In 2009, he was awarded the Scientific Merit and Excellence Award of the Portuguese AI Association. Scientific fact: 99% of TensorFlow users were not born when Carlos started working on Machine Learning.

title: DSPT#20 - Going with the (Data) flow...
description:
Today's massive amounts of data are in constant movement.

From creation to refinement and extensive analysis, there's a pulse, a flow of multimedia streams that carry invaluable information about users habits. Dealing with this type of data on the move requires a new perspective, that Prof. João Gama (http://www.liaad.up.pt/area/jgama/) is eager to share with us! João Gomes (https://www.linkedin.com/in/jgsousa/) will also put us up to date regarding Data Science latest developments at Farfetch, you don't want to miss it!

Oh, and please do enjoy our fantastic Chrismas Ale™, a.k.a. Beer!!

The meetup #20 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Wednesday, 13th December, 2017 around 18h30, at Farfetch - Rua da Lionesa nº 446, Edifício G12, 4465-671 Leça do Balio - Porto.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:
• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.
• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Machine Learning as it must be" by João Gama - University of Porto / Laboratory of Artificial Intelligence and Decision Support (LIAAD).
• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.
• 19:45-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break.
• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "TBA" by João Gomes - Farfetch.
• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers
• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!
-------------------------------------

Talk1: Machine Learning as it must be.

Abstract:
Nowadays, there are applications in which the data are modelled best not as persistent tables, but rather as transient data streams. In this keynote, we discuss the limitations of current machine learning and data mining algorithms. We discuss the fundamental issues in learning in dynamic environments like learning decision models that evolve over time, learning and forgetting, concept drift and change detection. Data streams are characterized by huge amounts of data that introduce new constraints in the design of learning algorithms: limited computational resources in terms of memory, processing time and CPU power. In this talk, we present some illustrative algorithms designed to taking these constraints into account. We identify the main issues and current challenges that emerge in learning from data streams, and present open research lines for further developments.

Short Bio:
João Gama is an Associate Professor at the University of Porto, Portugal. He is also a senior researcher and member of the board of directors of the Laboratory of Artificial Intelligence and Decision Support (LIAAD), a group belonging to INESC Porto.
João Gama serves as the member of the Editorial Board of Machine Learning Journal, Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery, Intelligent Data Analysis and New Generation Computing. He served as Co-chair of ECML 2005, DS09, ADMA09 and a series of Workshops on KDDS and Knowledge Discovery from Sensor Data with ACM SIGKDD. He was also the chair for the conference of Intelligent Data Analysis 2011.
His main research interest is in knowledge discovery from data streams and evolving data. He is the author of more than 200 papers reviewed by peers and author of a recent book on Knowledge Discovery from Data Streams. He has extensive publications in the area of data stream learning.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: The top 10 mistakes I did (do?) as a Data Scientist

Abstract:
The role of the Data Scientist is still relatively new. We are still collectively learning what it means to be one, what is expected of us and how best to deliver on the promise of the "unicorn" Data Scientist.

I've had the privilege of working on a top-tier, fast growing, Data Science team for the last 3 years. It's been a wonderful, crazy, often times painful experience, and to say I've learned alot is an understatement.

In this talk we will look back on some of the major mistakes I've made along the way. Maybe you can relate to some of them, or maybe not at all! We'll try to find that out.

Short Bio:
João Gomes is the Lead Data Scientist for the Recommendations team at Farfetch. Since joining the company in 2015 he has helped design and develop new algorithms that power personalized recommendations
and discoverability across the multiple channels of the Farfetch platform.
His team’s mission is to leverage large amounts of data to know, understand and ultimately inspire its customers.

His personal pet passions are python, pandas, spark, porto (fc), porto (wine) and the inappropriate use of alliterations. He holds a PhD in computational physics from the University of Aveiro, Portugal.

title: DSPT#19 - The Map that Leads to... Data Science!
description:
Feeling lost everytime you hear someone wispering about AI and its fabulous treasures? Wondering where could you learn more about Machine Learning and the ways it can improve your daily life?

Fear no more! We have the answer, and we'll share it with you... for a price: your attendance at our next Data Science Meetup in Coimbra!

Come aboard our pirate ship, meet other fellow Data Science swashbucklers and set sail! It's "Critical" that you become part of this adventure! "Critical" See what I did there? Lousy joke...? Well, at least rest assured that our speakers Evgheni Polisciuc and Pedro Verruma are experts on Data Science and not comedian salesmen...

Oh, and I'd enjoy our beers, you never know if it'll be The Last Beer's™...

The meetup #19 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Tuesday, 5th December, 2017 around 18h30, at IPN (Instituto Pedro Nunes) - Coimbra.

===== SCHEDULE ======

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and Data Science quiz.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Dynamic Cartography – from basics of Information Visualization to Adaptive Representation" by Evgheni Polisciuc - University of Coimbra.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:46-20:15: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2: "Critical AI: Problems and Solutions" by Pedro Verruma - Critical Software.

• 20:55: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Dynamic Cartography – from basics of Information Visualization to Adaptive Representation.

Abstract:
Techniques and methods provided by the field of information visualization should simplify our lives in multiple ways and in various situations. From complex analysis, such as buying behavior of clients in a big supermarket chain, or the concurrence among its superficies, to a simple sharing of the morning running trail, it is inevitable to rely on existing visualization techniques. But, even with the constant growth and integration of visualization in our daily work, many people are still having difficulties in identifying which visualization model should be chosen and how to apply it on a given dataset. Moreover, when dealing with geo-referenced data, the problem becomes even harder, due to the complexity of visualizing fixed or moving geographic phenomena. This talk demystifies complex concepts and taxonomies used in information visualization, focusing on geographic visualization.

Short Bio:
Evgheni Polisciuc is a data visualisation specialist and a cross-media designer. Currently he is a PhD student at the Doctoral Program for Information Science and Technology of University of Coimbra. He is interested in information visualization and aesthetics, visual complexity and perception and dynamic and adaptive visualization systems. Particularly, interested in the application of design strategies and visualization techniques to study of geo-temporal information.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Critical AI: Problems and Solutions

Abstract:
This talk will focus on how AI is being used at Critical Software and how it solves some specific problems such as: energy management, modeling ship behavior, telecom usage prediction, etc. For each case we will talk about how the problem was modeled and the implemented solution, with some numbers and details to give context. The idea is to share some knowledge about how we, at Critical, tackle AI and ML problems on deployed projects with significant dimension.

Short Bio:
My name is Pedro Verruma, I’m a 33 year old software engineer from Caldas da Rainha but living in Coimbra since 2002.

I started working at Wizdee in 2009, where I finish my masters degree from University of Coimbra. During my time at Wizdee I learned a lot about NLP, CBR, information extraction and other AI approaches. Couple of years later I moved to Guberni where I help developing monitoring solutions for IT and other types of equipments. In 2016 I got back to Wizdee to help with the development of CBR and NLP systems. At the moment I’m at Critical Software in the newly created AI team.

title: DSPT#18 - The Big Data Championship
description:
The crowd is really on fire as we approach another Data Science Portugal event!

We'll have another exciting match, this time between Altice Labs and University of Aveiro! On one side, the Big Data platform architeture players feel confident, betting on answering business needs with quick answers, flexibility and performance. On the other side, the trick will be combining machine learning with distributed systems do overcome the adversary and ensure victory on what we expect to be a chaotic game indeed!

Flags with "Vamos lá CAMBADA!" are spread all across the stadium, showing support for the team! Team captains Mário Moreira (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/227648046/) and Ricardo Dias (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/240284505/) are definitely on their best shape!

Join us at Aveiro and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™! First timers: welcome your new Beer overlords!

The meetup #18 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal)is going to take place on Wednesday, 22nd November, 2017 around 18h30, at Altice LABS - Rua Eng.º José Ferreira Pinto Basto, 3810 Aveiro.

======= SCHEDULE =======

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Big Data platform architecture at Altice LABS" by Mário Moreira - Altice LABS.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:45-20:10: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:10-20:40: Talk 2: "Can robots play soccer?" by Ricardo Dias - University of Aveiro.

• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com.

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Big Data platform architecture at Altice LABS.

Abstract:
Everybody is talking about Big Data, and with the related technologies available, it's easy to start experiment. However, when moving into production and when dealing with very large volumes of data and many data sources, things start to get complex and to answer to business needs within a short time frame it's important to have a flexible and performant platform architecture.

Short Bio:
Mário Moreira obtained a master's degree in Computer Science from the University of Minho in 1995 and a Bachelor's degree in Systems and Computer Engineering from the University of Minho in 1993. He joined the Center for Telecommunications Studies (later PT Inovação and now Altice Labs) in 1993 where he was developing several expert systems based on techniques of Artificial Intelligence. He was initially involved in several European research projects ACTS and Eurescom. He has worked in the area of network management services, having collaborated with the Portugal Telecom team within the framework of Expo98, and was involved in the development of several PT Inovação products in this area. In 2003 he was responsible for the Platform, Services and Applications Unit for Mobile Networks, where he was responsible for the development teams of various telecommunications systems, such as Unified Messaging, SMS-C, MMSC, AAA, Location and Portals Plataforms. He is currently Manager of the Experimentation and Technology Coordination Division in Altice Labs, and since 2012 is responsible for the BigData team of Altice Labs, which has been involved in the experimentation, evaluation and construction of prototypes involving these technologies as well as the operation of MEO's BigData production platform from which critical KPIs are processed and extracted for the television business and SAPO portal. In addition, he is also responsible for the Cloud team at Altice Labs that has been working on the evaluation and selection of cloud technologies, as well as the architecture, installation, configuration and operation of a cloud platform for internal use at Altice Labs.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Can robots play soccer?

Abstract:
From chess to autonomous driving, we have seen an increasing amount of applications where robots have to deal with tasks that are more and more challenging. One thing is clear: robots are moving from very controlled and previously-known environments to more unstructured ones. If precisely manipulating chess pieces poses its challenges, imagine how difficult it is to deal with a chaotic, rolling soccer ball while coordinating with the team-mates to beat an opponent team with exactly the same goals. In this interactive session, we will discuss the challenges, technologies and methods used in the development of CAMBADA - a top team currently participating in the major international robotic soccer competition.

Short Bio:
Ricardo Dias completed his M.Sc. in 2014 in Electronics and Telecommunications Engineering at University of Aveiro and is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in Computer Engineering at the same institution. In his research, he has been mainly interested in intelligent distributed systems and artificial intelligence. He is the team-leader of an autonomous robotics soccer team and has developed routing protocols for delay-tolerant data in vehicular environments. His main research interests are robotics research in integration, sensor fusion and multi-agent coordination.

title: DSPT#17 - I, For One, Welcome Our New Cyber Overlords!
description:
Feeling watched everytime you use your computer?
Getting the chills everytime someone brings the "AI will rule the world" topic at lunch? Attend the next Data Science Meetup and you'll be surprised to be... just about right!

Yep, from Predictive Maintenance to Customer Relationship, data scientists and engineers are working hard to feed machine learning systems with tons of data and improve infrastucture reliability and user journeys throughout essential systems for our everyday lives.

Hélder Ribeiro, Gonçalo Pereira, Nuno Paiva and Nuno Cravino are eager to endulge us with their experience on these topics, you don't want to miss this! Join us for another inspiring event at Porto and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™!

First timers: there's no end to our beer supplies! ;-)

The meetup #17 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Thursday, 9th November, 2017 around 18h30, at Euronext - Av. da Boavista 3433, 4100-138 Porto (https://maps.google.com/?q=Av.+da+Boavista+3433,+4100-138+Porto&entry=gmail&source=g).

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1 - "Predictive Maintenance in Cyber-Physical systems" by Hélder Ribeiro & Gonçalo Pereira - Nomad Tech.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2 - "A quick view on the analytical metamorphosis of a Telecom Operator" by Nuno Paiva & Nuno Cravino - NOS.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Predictive Maintenance in Cyber-Physical systems.

Abstract:

With the arrival of Digitalisation and of Industry 4.0, the use of IoT and Cyber Physical Systems technologies has become increasingly common in distinct areas like the railways or manufacturing industries, for different purposes like operation and maintenance.
In this talk, we present a project developed by Nomad Tech where the data collected from Fly London’s shoe factory cyber physical systems is used to create a predictive model capable of detect upcoming failures in the factory shopfloor components. We discuss our experiences with this project, what were the major challenges faced, results achieved and lessons learned.

Short Bios:

Hélder Ribeiro is head of Software Development at Nomad Tech. Graduated in Computer Science for the Engineering School – Porto University (FEUP), with an ERASMUS exchange period at INSA-Lyon, France. Post-Graduated in Business Intelligence and Analytics in Porto Business School (PBS). Having started professional activity in 2001, has been working in Software Engineering Project Management, Remote/Online Condition Monitoring (on-shore and on-board software applications) and Predictive Maintenance, for projects in Portugal, the Netherlands, UK, Norway, Germany and Australia.

Gonçalo Pereira holds a master in Computer Engineering from University of Porto (UP). He worked for a year as a data science researcher Faculty of Engineering of UP field before joining Nomad Tech where he has being working for nearly two years as a Data Scientist.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: A quick view on the analytical metamorphosis of a Telecom Operator.

Abstract:

Telco operators are struggling to become fully digital, overcoming their traditional way to do business ingrained in rigid infrastructure to embrace the chaos and lightspeed of the digital age. For this exciting journey, it’s fundamental a sound analytical strategy taking advantage of existing infrastructure, data and trusted Customer relationship. To stay relevant, we need to produce comprehensive Customer knowledge through fast analytics and capacity to deploy operationally embedding optimization in all Customer touchpoints.
On this talk we’ll give an overview of the journey, pain points and one use case of how we’re deploying analytics contributing to transform the slow caterpillar into a digital butterfly!

Short Bios:

Nuno Paiva is the head of Consumer Analytics at NOS. He has more than 8 years working in Telecom, focusing on Marketing & Customer Value Management, and has also worked previously at Accenture as a Senior Consultant.
At the moment he leads a Data Ninjas team that are enabling NOS to make better decisions using the latest analytical artillery.
He’s also Chief Dad Officer of adorable three year old Rita!
In his spare time he’s the family Cook, a music digger and an avid learner - just self-taught piano classes and finishing Stanford’s Andrew NG Deep Learning Specialization.
Nuno holds one MSC in Eletrotechical Engineering (University of Coimbra) and another Msc of Data Analytics (University of Porto).

Nuno Cravino is a Data Scientist doing Consumer Analytics at NOS in his first journey into the corporate world after a few years doing learning, researching, and working inside academia and start ups as a statistician/computer scientist/database designer.
Nowadays he focus mainly in the development and operationalization of statistical/mathematical models predicting expected behavior and consumer characteristics.
In his free time he enjoys playing around with microcontrollers, following the new developments on type theory and functional programming languages, reading some sci-fi, and drinking a good ale.
Nuno holds a BSc. In Philosophy (University of Oporto), a BSc. In Computer Science (University of Oporto), and concluded post-graduated studies in Computational Statistics (Universidade Aberta).

title: DSPT#16 - Data Science Inception: breaking stuff, one window at a time
description:
In a world of information flood, data leakages and natural stupidity overall, a hero rises: Data Science Portugal!

This special edition is powered by Microsoft, embracing Satya Nadella's vision of the wonders of Machine Learning.

Oh great, another AI-first Meetup... wait, this one is the Real Deal™! But you'll need to see it for yourself...

Stay ahead of the competition, strengthen your data skills and meet fellow data natives (scientists, engineers and unicorns overall)! One thing's for sure: you will not be disappointed!

The meetup #16 of Data Science Portugal ( https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal ) is going to take place on Tuesday, 7th November, 2017 around 18h30, at Microsoft - Rua do Fogo de Santelmo Lote 2.07.02, 1990-110 Lisboa.

=========== SCHEDULE =========

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1 - "7 Secrets of Effective Data Science in a Business Context" by Rui Carmo - Microsoft.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:45-20:14: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:15-20:45: Talk 2 - "Data Science in the wild - what they don't teach you in books" by Pedro Bizarro - Feedzai.

• 20:55: Closing, hanging out and some beers.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: 7 Secrets of Effective Data Science in a Business Context

Abstract:

This talk will take hard-earned lessons from field engagements where technology was flawless but people, in a nutshell, screwed up royally, and address how to scale up your data science team and methods in a way that ensures you can go from a simple model that shows promise to a reliable, reproducible process the business that hired you can rely upon. In the process, we will explore essential skills that every data scientist should be aware of and learn to work with alongside.

Short Bio:

Rui Carmo is a Cloud Solution Architect at Microsoft working in the Data & AI domain, with an Information Systems and Computer Engineering degree from IST, a number of faded business and industry certifications and over twenty years’ experience in technology management, large scale distributed systems and operational analytics, both from an implementer and a decision makers’ perspective. He currently spends most of his time doing technology transfer and cloud enablement, and is usually called in to fix stuff someone else designed broken.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Data Science in the wild - what they don't teach you in books

Abstract:

Data science and machine learning books focus on statistical analysis, data imputation and cleaning, sampling, feature engineering, overfitting and underfitting, bias and variance, models, and metrics and more. At Feedzai we discovered that all those are the easier tasks and concepts and that there are a few very important things that books don't mention that can affect results dramatically. We summarize our discoveries and share them as lessons for the benefit of others.

Short-bio:

Pedro Bizarro is co-founder and Chief Science Officer of Feedzai. Drawing on a history in academia and research, Pedro has turned his technical expertise into entrepreneurial success as he has helped to develop Feedzai’s industry-leading artificial intelligence platform to fight fraud. Pedro has been an official member of the Forbes Technology Council, a visiting professor at Carnegie Mellon University, a Fulbright Fellow, and has worked with CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research. Pedro holds a Computer Science PhD from the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

title: DSPT#15 - Adapting to survive in a Post-Datalyptic World...
description:
We've all heard about Evolution. It's just the same with data: from simple log parsing to powerful predictive analytics, from Calculus to Data Science, from statisticians to machine learning engineers. Paulo Cortez, Professor at University of Minho, will walks us through adaptive Business Intelligence and intelligent data analysis!

And if you get a little thirsty during this journey, enjoy some H20 analytics with the help of Serafim Pinto, CTO of Performetric. Surf's up on the Big Data ocean of insights! >=]

Join us for another event at Braga and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™! First timers: beer is your best friend!

The meetup #15 of Data Science Portugal ( https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal ) is going to take place on Thursday, 18th October, 2017 around 18h30, at Subvisual - Avenida D. João II 404, 4º andar, Room 45, 4715-275 Braga.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Adaptive Business Intelligence - An Introduction with Application Examples" by Paulo Cortez, Professor at UMinho.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2: "Scalable Machine Learning with H2O" by Serafim Pinto, CTO of Performetric.

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups?

Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Adaptive Business Intelligence - An Introduction with Application Examples.

Abstract:

Due to advances in Information and Communication technology, nowadays it is easy to collect, store and process data. The interest in such intelligent data analysis is increasing and has been approached under slight distinct perspectives, such as machine learning, business intelligence and data science. This talk will briefly introduce the concept of Adaptive Business Intelligence, which adopts predictive data mining and modern optimization for decision support, and present examples of its value in real-world applications.

Short Bio:

Paulo Cortez (PhD in Computer Science) is Associate Professor at the Department of Information Systems, University of Minho, and Coordinator of the Information Systems and Technologies (IST) research group of ALGORITMI Centre. With more than 20 years of experience in Intelligent Data Analysis, he is co-author of more than 130 indexed publications in international peer reviewed journals. He is also author of the Modern Optimization with R book.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Scalable Machine Learning with H2O.

Abstract:

How to use high-performance machine learning with your favorite programming language to solve today’s most challenging business problems based on the H2O platform. H2O is an open source, in-memory, distributed, fast, and scalable machine learning and predictive analytics platform that allows you to build machine learning models on big data and provides easy productionalization of those models in an enterprise environment.

Short Bio:

Serafim Pinto is the CTO and Co-Founder of Performetric. His greatest interests are the technology and software development in order to improve the quality of life. An entrepreneur with creative spirit and passionate about Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. Obsessed with performance in software development.
He obtained bachelor’s in Informatics Engineering from the University of Minho.

title: DSPT#14 - I Can Haz Data Science job?! KThxBye!
description:
Coimbra is most charming... whenever there's a Data Science Portugal event! :)

You've known it all along: big data drives your passion, you've completed all the MOOC's on Data Science, your Github account is full of Machine Learning side projects, your Deep Learning Fu is strong!! Time to get that Data Scientist job you've dreamed about for so long! And who better to provide you than Miguel Almeida, leading research at Feedzai, one of most well known Data Science focused companies?

Speaking on evolving, how about dealing cards during that interview with the help of Evolutionary Algorithms? António Leitão, from CISUC, will talk about his neo-Darwinian ideas with Genetic Programming and explain his PhD work to us, mere mortals! Or should I say, soon-to-be Data Scientists? >=]

Join us for this season's premiere event at Coimbra and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™! First timers: we've restocked our beer supplies! ;)

The meetup #14 of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Tuesday, October 3rd, 2017 around 18h30, at Instituto Pedro Nunes - R. Pedro Nunes, 3030-199 Coimbra, in the cafeteria of Block C.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "How To Interview Data Scientists" by Miguel Almeida - Feedzai.

19:40-19:45: Group photo.

19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee Break.

20:00-20:30: Talk 2: "Evolutionary Algorithms for Data Science 101" by António Leitão - CISUC.

20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers.

21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: How To Interview Data Scientists.

Abstract: "Data Scientist" is one of the hottest job titles right now, and therefore, interviewing them is also a skill in high demand. Since it is a very new skill set, it is also a hiring process new to many companies. In this talk, I will share some practical lessons I learned from being at hundreds of Data Science interviews at Feedzai. I will provide four concrete guidelines on how to interview Data Scientists, including real (anonymous) examples.

Short Bio: Miguel has a Master's in Physics from Instituto Superior Técnico, in Lisbon (IST), and a dual-degree PhD in Computer Science from Aalto University, Finland, and IST. He has 5 years as a Data Scientist in the industry, 2 of which at Feedzai Research. He is now the leader of the Research team which now has 9 people, and counting.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Evolutionary Algorithms for Data Science 101.

Abstract: Evolutionary Algorithms are adaptation procedures based on Darwin's theory of natural selection and Mendelian genetic inheritance. They were first developed by Holland and have had a double role, on the one hand as a means to study biological behaviour, and on the other hand as optimization and search algorithms. They have since gradually been applied to multiple research fields from chemistry to astrophysics, aerospace engineering, and pattern recognition and data mining. Their use with data is somewhat emerging, with many ideas floating around regarding their usefulness. This talk aims at introducing Genetic Algorithms and Genetic Programming, discussing established applications on multiple data related tasks, discussing limitations, but mostly bringing up open questions that still seek answers.

Short Bio: António Leitão is a researcher at CISUC, University of Coimbra. As an AI researcher and enthusiast, he likes everything bio-inspired and has been working on means to incorporate (some) neo-Darwinian ideas into Evolutionary Algorithms. Studying the impact of nature-motivated non-traditional selection operators in the fields of Genetic Algorithms and Genetic Programming has been the bulk of his PhD thesis, which is in its final stages. In 2016 António joined Hoko Ltd. where he worked for one year as an AI expert, mostly dealing with NLP tasks.

title: DSPT#13 - Data on the Move!
description:
You know that feeling: sunny beaches behind, no more waves, rainy days are coming... time to get back to work! What could possibly shine a ray of hope during these dreadful times? Why, a Data Science Portugal Meetup of course! We're back on track(s)!

This time, you'll learn how Movvo is understanding customer behavior and visitor interactions while shopping, using predictive analytics together with geographic information and consumer history. Also, you'll be able to watch how Veniam is engineering a mesh network to increase network capacity and lower communication costs, researching potential alternatives to connect users on the move.

Join us for this season's premiere event and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™! First timers: we've restocked our beer supplies! 😉

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:45-20:10: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups?

Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Data Science in the Internet of Moving Things.

Abstract:

Veniam is changing the concept that computer and sensor networks are made from static nodes connected by permanent links. We see vehicles as moving network nodes that connect with other vehicles, access points, user devices and sensors. We see vehicles having multiple communication technologies that can be combined to increase network capacity and to lower communication costs. We see an Internet of Moving Things. Within this context, Data Science plays a key role: making the right questions, providing answers based on data and, thus, supporting and help driving business and engineering. In this presentation, we will show some of the challenges a Data Science team is exposed in such a disruptive company and how we are addressing them.

Short Bio:

Daniel Moura joined Veniam on September 2015 and is the Director of Data Analytics, leading the Data Science team. PhD in Informatics by University of Porto (2011), Daniel has 11 years of experience in data analysis, 17 years in computer science and engineering, 7 years as teaching assistant on computer science subjects, 9 publications in peer-reviewed journals, 3 international patents, and several papers in international conferences. Daniel was Visiting Researcher in the University College of London (England), the University of Gent (Belgium) and the École Polytechnique de Montréal (Canada). Before joining Veniam, Daniel was Research Associate at University of Porto, where he was responsible for data analysis in the FP7 Future Cities project.

-------------------------------------
Talk2: The movvers of data.

Abstract:

How can people make decisions based on data, for example know the number of visitors to a city and the time they spend visiting it? This information may be useful but if you can go further and set tribes and predict the future so you can prepare tomorrow, next week and next month. With this information you can make reinforcement in the staff that does the cleaning, set a marketing event to influence a tribe, attract new tribes to your shopping or explore even more the ones you have already. The main goal is transform data into knowledge. Data is available and the use of simple KPI its out of fashion and anyone can do it, the novelty is to transform and analyze it with models (machine learning and time series). Movvo is a leading visitor behavior intelligence. Our customers can understand the relationship between each brand in its space - the brands that have high levels of cross-shopping, the order in which stores are visited and the amount of time spent in each -, they can analyze how visitors interact with shopping: where they go; what they do; how long they stay; and how often they come back. Predictions to help customers prepare the next day, week or month and join people in a group in such a way that people in the same group are more similar to each other than to those in other groups.

Short Bio:

Miguel Arantes holds a master in Mathematics Engineering from University of Porto. He has been working in statistical models for over 7 years in credit institutions and IT companies. His priority are is family (2 loving kids), plays Pokemon GO and applies mathematics also to the game.

title: DSPT#12 - Thought you were going on vacations? Think again!
description:
Beautiful beaches, the sun shining, crystal clear waters and the sound of waves... What could be better than that? Well, a Data Science Portugal Meetup, of course! And we got just the thing: two Data Science Gods eager to ruin your vacation plans! You’re welcome! ;-)

This time, our focus is on Natural Language Processing, combining the power of the crowd with the capabilities of Neural Networks! Drink the knowledge of Unbabel, surf the research waves of Amazon and swim in the ocean of Data Science! Join us for this refreshing event and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™!

First timers: we. still. have. beer! 😆

For all the persevering members of our community, we may have some Hawaian drinks to spice things up!! (p < 0.05) 😎

The twelfth meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Friday, 16th June, 2017 around 18h30, at UPTEC - Polo Tecnológico (https://maps.google.com/maps?q=461%20Rua%20Alfredo%20Allen%2C%20Porto).

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and Data Science quiz.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "How to combine AI with the crowd to scale professional-quality translation" by João Graça, CTO of Unbabel.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:46-20:00: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:05-20:35: Talk 2: "Going Neurotic with Neural Word Embeddings" by Luís Sarmento (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/129956062/), former Lead of Research Teams at Amazon.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com.

Oh, and one more thing: we know you really wanted to enjoy this “bridge” but… who cares?? >:-]!
See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: How to combine AI with the crowd to scale professional-quality translation.

Unbabel is accelerating the shift towards a world without language barriers by enabling trustworthy, seamless and scalable translations between companies and their customers.In this talk we will show how we combine different Machine Learning techniques together with a crowd of non-professional translators and achieve professional-quality translations in an unprecedented speed and scale.

Short Bio:

João Graça is currently the CTO of Unbabel. He was previously a research scientist at Dezine and Flashgroup. João did his PhD in Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning at IST and Upenn. He is the author of several papers, his main research topics are machine learning with side information, unsupervised learning and machine translation. João is one of the co-founders of the Lisbon Machine Learning Summer School (LxMLS).

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Going Neurotic with Neural Word Embeddings.

Abstract: TBA

Short Bio:

Luis Sarmento (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/129956062/) holds a PhD in Computer Science from University of Porto (2010), with background in Electrical Engineering (Bs+MsC) and Artificial Intelligence (MsC). He has been working in the fields of Natural Language / Search for about 15 years, both as a member of research groups at the University of Porto and in industry. In 2010 he joined Portugal Telecom / SAPO as tech lead for Big-Data and Recommender Systems, and in 2012 he joined Amazon to lead research teams in the fields of Query Understanding and Voice Shopping. In 2017, and after 7 years in industry, Luis is taking a sabbatical year to study topics located in the interface between Machine Learning and Abstract Algebra. He will also be writing in his blog (https://imaginaryaxis.wordpress.com/) about random topics in mathematics and he will be practicing his futurologist skills by speculating about AI in his LinkedIn feed.

title: DSPT#11-Great balls of fire!Where?Where's it?Where's the needle in the haystack?
description:
Great balls of fire! Prepare yourself! Clear your agenda! May is only in the middle and we have another DSPT meetup in the horizon!

This time get your magnifier out of the box, clean the lens and prepare to spot for the needle in the middle of the haystack. This is what we will learn with Raquel Ribeiro (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/195806651/) who is going to teach us to spot fraud in the middle of a huge amount of Credit Card Transactions. But don't worry because she won't be alone. Raquel will be followed by Claudio Rebelo de Sá (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/206314455/), who will teach us a bit about how to check for outliers when dealing with networks of data.

Join us and embark on another great voyage powered by B.E.E.R! Yes... our fuel is that good!!

P.S. Don't wait for tomorrow! Register now because we predict these tickets will go like HOT BUNS!!!

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and Data Science quiz.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: “Exposing fraud with machine learning” with Raquel Ribeiro (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/195806651/), Senior Data Scientist at Feedzai.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:46-20:05: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:05-20:35: Talk 2: "Discovering the unusual with Data Mining" with Cláudio Rebelo de Sá (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/206314455/), Postdoc at INESC.

• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us toinfo@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk 1: Exposing fraud with machine learning

Currency can take many shapes, from physical coins to credit cards. By 2020, global card fraud is forecasted to exceed 35 billion dollars, and can affect virtually anyone in the world. From a machine learning perspective, detecting and preventing fraud is a spectacular problem for a number of reasons. First, whether the customer is withdrawing money from an ATM or purchasing goods online, a decision to let the transaction go through should be made in a split of a second. Second, fraudsters are very smart and can quickly make our models obsolete, even if they have just been deployed. Third, fraud to legit transactions can appear in a 1:10 to 1:100000 ratio, making this an extreme unbalancement problem. And finally, feature engineering needs to happen in real time.

In this talk I will discuss how we face these challenges at Feedzai and apply machine learning techniques in venues as varied as banks and online retailers. We use a symbiosis of the traditional rules system and machine learning techniques which detect fraud patterns in the data. Feedzai’s disruptive technology allows us to harvest details about a given transaction and connect it to previous ones while the machine learning network gathers patterns and delivers a decision on whether the transaction should be allowed to go through. This all needs to happen in real-time so that the customer’s experience is not negatively affected, whilst effectively keeping fraud at bay.

Short-bio

Raquel is a senior data scientist at Feedzai based in Lisbon. With a Physics degree from the University of Porto, she went to Cambridge in the UK to earn a Masters of Advanced Studies in Mathematics and a PhD in Theoretical Physics. Her thesis was on decoding subtle patterns drawn in the microwave sky from the oldest light in the universe. She did postdoctoral work on models of dark energy in the US and in the UK, before rendering to the world of Data Science. Raquel has worked as a data scientist for a start up in London, leading the research in her team into forecasting and market segmentation for various businesses and public services. After 10 years abroad, she accepted the challenge to return home to work at Feedzai fighting fraud with the Data Science ninjas.

------------------------------------

Talk 2: "Discovering the unusual with Data Mining"

In this talk, I will give an overview on Subgroup Discovery and some of its applications. Subgroup Discovery is a data mining technique that searches for groups, within a population, which show some exceptional behavior. This approach has been used to study different topics such as Fraud Detection or Preference Learning.

Short-Bio

I have a PhD degree in Computer Science from the University of Leiden (Netherlands). My research topics are Preference Learning, Association Rule Mining and Subgroup Discovery. Currently I work in data mining applications for different industries, such as pharmaceutical companies, retailers and electronics companies.

title: DSPT#10 - Big Bang Theory: Creating and compressing data with Machine Learning!
description:
Can you feel it? All that magical energy that surrounds us...?

No...? Neither can we, because we're Data Scientists and deal with serious scientific matters, not that exoteric data wizardry mumbo-jumbo! ;-)

When we want to deal with energy, we turn to the real stuff, that is, thermotechnology! And that's exactly what Tânia Correia (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/221710111/) will talk about. From sensors to data storage, from processing to analysis, practical solutions for everyday problems regarding IoT and Data Science teams!

But because DS isn't only about creating huge amounts of data, sometimes we also need to understand information by compressing it and finding similarities among groups. That's the case with using Machine Learning for analysing DNA intel. How's your Markov skill set?

If you need you still have time to check a bit about it! If not, come any way! Join us for this amazing event and become part of the Data Science Portugal Community™!

First timers: we. still. have. beer! ;-)

Oh, who are we kidding, there are probably no first-timers, it's the 10th DSPT event after all! And we predict you'll be there! Help us, think of the AUC, be a responsible data scientist and show up!

The tenth meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Wednesday, 10th May, 2017 around 18h30, at IEUA - Incubadora de Empresas da Universidade de Aveiro.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and Data Science quiz.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Heat up the data: a data science use case for thermotechnology" with Tânia Correia (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/221710111/), Data Engineer at Bosch Termotecnologia.

• 19:40-19:45: Group photo.

• 19:46-20:05: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:05-20:35: Talk 2: “Compression-based Machine Learning for DNA analysis” with Diogo Pratas (https://www.meetup.com/datascienceportugal/members/212586857/), Researcher at IEETA (http://wiki.ieeta.pt/wiki/index.php/Main_Page) (University of Aveiro).

• 20:50: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Heat up the data: a data science use case for thermotechnology.

The Internet of Things (IoT) allows us to gather data from physical events. We can choose which data to collect as well the sampling rate. All these decisions will influence the dataset available for the teams that will consume the data. Concerning the Data Science team, many challenges arise since the liability of the connection as the complexity and uncertainty of the data itself collected from sensors. In this talk we will present the journey since data collection until data analysis in the energy field.

Short Bio:

Tânia Correia is Data Engineer at Bosch Termotecnologia in Aveiro. She works with the Data Science team responsible for mining value from the connected heating appliances data. Previously, she has taken part of the project EU FP7 IoT.est as a Research and Development Engineer at Altice Labs, former PT Inovação.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Compression-based machine learning for DNA analysis

Data compressors are unsupervised Machine Learning methods that can measure the similarity of and between sequences. In this talk, we will describe a state-of-the-art compressor, based on a cooperation between multiple Markov and tolerant Markov models, for measure relative similarity. As a case study we will show its applicability in DNA sequences, namely for detection of genomic rearrangements, identification of metagenomic sample composition and reconstruction and analysis of ancient genomes, such as Neandertals.

Short Bio:

Diogo Pratas is a researcher at IEETA, at the University of Aveiro, in the areas of DNA compression and ancient DNA. His research interests are in the areas of information theory, data compression, genomics and ancient DNA, with over 30 papers published in peer-reviewed international journals and conferences. He received a Ph.D. in Computer Science (2016) from the University of Aveiro. He has worked for several private companies, namely on breaking network security (pentest) and developing Linux-based systems. He is an ARPA member of the SDF and a member of the APRP.

title: DSPT#9 - Perks of a Data Wizard:mastering the "R" cane & opening Pandora's box
description:
Put on your stylish robes, capes and staves (did you know Farfetch (https://www.farfetch.com) sells Dalaran Premium™ mage items? Go check it out!) and join us for another magical lesson from Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal/)! 😎

Teaching you the most powerful spells on the Data Science tome of knowledge, this gathering will dazzle your mind and pleasure your ears to the tunes of Music Information Retrieval and Data Exploration with R!

Be one of the first to attend this event, prepare for this journey and meet other fellow Data magicians, warlocks, spellcasters, tricksters... and Data Wizards in general! First timers: we...have...beer!

The nineth meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal/) is going to take place on Wednesday, 19th April, 2017 around 18h30, at Farfetch - Rua da Lionesa nº 446, Edifício G12, 4465-671 Leça do Balio - Porto.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-18:50: Welcome and networking.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Recommendation strategies for Music Discovery at Pandora" with Fabien Gouyon (https://www.linkedin.com/in/fgouyon/), Principal Scientist at Pandora (https://www.pandora.com/).

• 19:35-19:36: Group photo.

• 19:36-20:00: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2: "On-going Deep Learning projects at Farfetch" with Hugo Pinto and Fábio Pinto (https://www.linkedin.com/in/f%C3%A1bio-pinto-62203448/), Data Scientists at Farfetch.

• 20:35-21h05: Talk 3: "Data wrangling in the “tidyverse” with R" with Jim Porzak (https://www.linkedin.com/in/jimporzak/), co-founder of the San Francisco Bay Area R User Group (https://www.meetup.com/R-Users/)and co-organizer of the East Bay R Beginners meetup (https://www.meetup.com/r-enthusiasts/).

• 21h10 - Closing, hanging out and some beers

Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

=== TALKS ===

Talk1: Recommendation strategies for Music Discovery at Pandora.

Recommending what you like best?, or recommending what’s novel and you might like? This is a difficult question to answer in the context of recommending music to millions of listeners every day. In this seminar I will talk about our research on Recommender Systems and Music Information Retrieval in the Pandora internet radio, I’ll focus on the trade-off between “exploration" and “exploitation" in music recommendation. I’ll show examples of the diverse types of data we work with, introduce the Music Genome Project, and provide insights on the design of ensembles of recommenders. And we’ll listen to some music of course!

Short Bio:

Fabien Gouyon is Principal Scientist at the internet radio Pandora, where he does applied research on personalized music recommendation. He also currently serves as President of the International Society for Music Information Retrieval. His research interests are in Data Science and Music Information Retrieval, with over 90 papers published in peer-reviewed international journals and conferences. He received a PhD in Computer Science (2005) from the Pompeu Fabra University in Barcelona and did a Post-doc in the Austrian Research Institute for Artificial Intelligence in Vienna (2005-2007). He started and led the Sound and Music Computing Group at INESC TEC in Porto (2007-2014).

-------------------------------------

Talk2: On-going Deep Learning projects at Farfetch

Deep learning is a relatively new area of Machine Learning that has been achieving state-of-the-art results across several domains. As Data Scientists at Farfetch working directly with the Product team, it is our responsibility to embed our products with disruptive technology and enhance customer experience. In this talk, we will provide an overview of several on-going (and yet to come) projects in which Deep Learning methods play a major role, such as modeling fashion sense to provide recommendations of complementary products, improve the search engine through language modeling and enrich product descriptions with visual features.

Short Bio:

Hugo Pinto is a Data Scientist at Farfetch working with the Search Team in the areas of Natural Language Processing, Learning to Rank and Computer Vision. With background in Physics and Applied Maths (Dynamical Systems, Optimization and Control), he has been working in the past years in Algorithmic Trading and Modeling at stock exchange markets and in several research projects as a statistician consultant. His main research interests 'lie' in the fields of Statistical Learning and Modeling, Deep Learning, Probabilistic Graphical Models and Optimization Algorithms.
Fábio Pinto is a Data Scientist at Farfetch, a luxury fashion e-commerce platform, working on recommender systems to enhance online user experience at the Farfetch website. He is currently a PhD student in Machine Learning at the Engineering Faculty of the University of Porto, after finishing is MSc degree in Data Analysis and Decision Support Systems and graduating in Economics at the University of Minho. In the past, he has collaborated with several R&D projects, particularly on retail and predictive maintenance in an industrial scenario. His main research interests 'lie' in metalearning and automatic machine learning.

-------------------------------------

Talk3: Data wrangling in the “tidyverse” with R.

There is a revolution in the R community called the “tidyverse” instigated by Hadley Wickham, his students and co-workers at RStudio (Hadley is best known for his ggplot2 package which transformed data visualization in R). The workhouse in tidyverse is the dplyr package for efficiently doing data transformations. It is efficient in two senses: first, very efficient coding and debugging; secondly, it is fast. Under the hood, most of the work is done in C++ when working on local data. Larger data sets can reside on relational DBMS’s for which dplyr pushes up SQL to do most of the work. This is particularly effective when the data summarization is done on the server and dplyr only pulls down to R a very reduced set of data for final analysis and presentation. PostgreSQL and AWS Redshift are good targets for dplyr. This talk will be a quick introduction to dplyr (and friends like readr, tidyr, and tibble) with a few real-world examples from my recent projects. For a deep dive into tidyverse, dplyr, and much more, see the on-line version of Hadley Wickham & Garrett Grolemund’s R for Data Science at http://r4ds.had.co.nz/ .

Short Bio:

Jim Porzak is a (semi) retired data scientist living near Berkeley, California specializing in data-driven customer insights. He has been using R since 2002, is co-founder of the San Francisco Bay Area R User Group and co-organizer of the East Bay R Beginners meetup. Recent clients include Lynda.com, One Medical, Leitersburg Cinemas, and Li & Fung. Before retiring he did customer insights / BI work at, or for, Minted.com, Ancestry.com, Responsys, Sun Microsystems, LA Times, Apple, Chicago Sun Times, among others starting in 2003 at Loyalty Matrix. Jim is an active author and speaker both in the US and Europe. See his past presentations at DS4CI.org/Archives.

title: DSPT#8 - Detecting Outliers? Cracking Semantics?! Oh boy, you're in for a ride!
description:
"Bracaris"! No, this isn't a Game of Thrones episode, but just as epic: Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is arriving at Braga!

Bringing you the latest on what's being done on the Data Science sphere of possibilities, this meetup will surely answer a lot of questions regarding topics such as Anomaly Detection and Natural Language Processing.

Be one of the first to attend this event and be prepared to network with other fellow Data Scientists, Engineers, Architects, DataOps... and Unicorns in general! First timers: we. have. beer!

The eighth meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Wednesday, 5th April, 2017 around 18h30, at Startup Braga.

=== SCHEDULE ===

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and Networking.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Data Mining Anomaly Detection: Finding 'weirdness'" with João Brandão, Data and Artificial Intelligence Engineer at Robert Bosch.

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2: "Have we cracked semantics? A practitioner's exploration into what’s possible" with Daniel Loureiro, Head of Data Science at Followprice.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

=== TALKS ===

Talk1: Data Mining Anomaly Detection: Finding "weirdness".

As a data nerd one of most enthusiastic things to me is to find things that are not supposed to happen and learn new insights about the business with these unexpected outcomes. One possible way to do it is using anomaly detection techniques that can identify abnormal and unusual behaviors in the data, so on this talk, we will discuss better what it is an anomaly, an outlier, and in which problems these techniques can be applied.

We will also see how some of these techniques cover some common situations like:

- You don’t have much data about these weird events.
- These historical events that you analyzed occurred for different reasons.
- You have unlabeled data- The data don’t follow a statistical distribution
- You have event-based data
- You have a real-time need

It will be an overview on how can statistics and machine learning help us with this type of tasks that are common on domains like Fraud, Intrusion Detection, Process monitoring, etc.

Short Bio:

João Brandão is a Data and Artificial Intelligence Engineer at Robert Bosch in Braga, where multimedia devices are developed and produced for use by the best known car brands. João is working on projects related to Industry 4.0, by creating Data-Driven products on Business Intelligence and Machine Learning that use manufacturing data content to predict future results and detect unusual events.

João previously worked on Business Intelligence at Farfetch for 3 years, while obtaining his MSc Degree in Informatics Engineering at Minho University with a focus on Business Intelligence and Intelligent Systems.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Have we cracked semantics? A practitioner's exploration into what’s possible.

Words are complex things. Much beyond tokens in a string, they convey meanings and ideas that rely on your past knowledge and experience to be understood. With the arrival of word2vec in 2013, Data Scientists took a huge step in making some of that understanding available to machines.

We’ll start by looking at different word embedding techniques, such as word2vec, and how they capture relations such as king - man + woman = queen. Afterwards, we’ll get to play with them on a Jupyter Notebook and demonstrate how they can be used for search and recommendation in your own projects.

Short Bio:

Daniel Loureiro is currently Head of Data Science at Followprice, where he’s working on automatic product categorisation and non-collaborative recommendation. While finishing his degree in CS, he created his first startup (PepFeed) focused on information retrieval, and recently sold it to Followprice. He’s also an aspiring open-source contributor working on tools to help democratize data and industry-insights.

title: DSPT #7 - From Machine Learning safety reminders to Lambda Architecture
description:
Hello guys! So here we are with the next event. We hope that you are ready to learn a bit about what's being done in the Portuguese Industry related to Data Science!!

So, the seventh meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Wednesday, 22nd March, 2017, 18h30, at IEUA - Incubadora de Empresas da Universidade de Aveiro.

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and Data Science quiz.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Fasten your seat belt first & other safety reminders for Data Science & Machine Learning" with Rui Quintino, Senior Architect & Data Fellow at DevScope.

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee Break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2: "Lean Big Data In Growth Stage Startup" with João Ascenção, Data Scientist at UniPlaces.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking.

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Fasten your seat belt first & other safety reminders for Data Science & Machine Learning

In the age of Big Data, Internet of Things, Deep Learning, increasingly effective Cognitive & Artificial Intelligence, we seem to have technology & computational resources for unlimited data mining. Still, extracting valuable insights from data remains a real challenge.

We'll tackle some of the typical issues & obstacles we face when trying to extract meaning & insights from observational data. The never ending data quality challenges, why correlation is not causation is just the start… Why it's so hard to extract real causes & factors from data, and... what is observational data anyway? Things you'll be glad to know before presenting your first data project insights.

Short Bio:

Rui Quintino is Senior Architect & Data Fellow at DevScope and works with Microsoft data related technologies since 1998. Currently he works on Data Research & Advanced Analytics team at DevScope, learning and evaluating data science frameworks & languages and how they can fit & bring added value to DevScope products & SaaS solutions offerings.

Beyond data technologies, Rui also has professional experience in web, mobile & security and he likes to mix all of these to make the world a better place through the use of data.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Lean Big Data In Growth Stage Startups

Uniplaces is a two-sided, growing marketplace for Student accommodation.

In order to build decisive competitive advantage, areas such as matching, search, pricing and retention are key, but require substantial volume and variety of data, alongside low latency and fault tolerance requirements.

To address these issues and build the right foundation, the team decided for the Lambda Architecture, but avoiding Hadoop as centerpiece.

We will go through an implementation using AWS S3 as the main storage instead of HDFS and Apache Spark processing (batch and stream) and machine learning.

Complementary tooling includes AWS Kinesis, Scala/Akka, scheduling, plus a SQL engine for BI, visualization and ad-hoc analysis.

We will see how such setup can help growing stage startups building leaner data services and teams.

Short Bio:

João Ascensão is a Data Scientist at Uniplaces, a marketplace for student accommodation and a global student brand, with an Economics degree from Nova SBE, and subsequent studies in Development Economics at ISEG Lisbon. He is part of the Data team at Uniplaces, the data science, research and data engineering squad, part of the Product, Engineering and Design (PED) division. He joined Uniplaces about 2 years ago, after 4 years crunching data in the Finance and FinTech industries. He is currently working on foundation projects such as the new data infrastructure, as well as studying and improving existing marketplace dynamics, using data science, machine learning and some economic theory, especially search, discovery and two-sided matching models.

title: DSPT #6 - From Deep Learning to Data Science in the wild... wait... what??
description:
Hello data science enthusiasts! In the next meetup we will focused on how to manage a data science team and see what people are doing with or in deep learning. Are you ready to learn more and discuss about this interesting subjects in this field? 😎

So, the sixth meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/groups/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Tuesday, 21st February, 2017, 18h30, at UPTEC - Polo Tecnológico (http://uptec.up.pt/en) (auditorium).

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and Data Science quiz.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "Deep Learning: Variations on the Theme and Current Trends" with Jaime Cardoso, Associate Professor at FEUP and Lead at INESC.

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2: "Data Science in the wild: the good, the bad and (mostly) the ugly" with Luís Sarmento, former Lead Research Teams at Amazon.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking (http://doodle.com/poll/27rhu4zkxw9xrwuc)

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there! 😎

-------------------------------------

Talk1: Deep Learning: Variations on the Theme and Current Trends

When we look at the brain, we see many levels of processing. It is believed that each level is learning features or representations at increasing levels of abstraction. This observation has inspired a recent trend in machine learning known as deep learning, which attempts to replicate this kind of architecture in a computer. In this talk, we give a brief overview of this field. After discussing the need for deep architectures, we will review some of the key models in the field, including deep generative models, deep neural networks, stacked autoencoders, and deep kernel methods.

Since the topic of deep learning is currently evolving very quickly, we will finalized by discussing the most promising research directions.

Short Bio:
Jaime S. Cardoso holds a Licenciatura (5-year degree) in Electrical and Computer Engineering in 1999, an MSc in Mathematical Engineering in 2005 and a Ph.D. in Computer Vision in 2006, all from the University of Porto. Cardoso is an Associate Professor with Habilitation at the Faculty of Engineering of the University of Porto (FEUP) and the leader of the ‘Information Processing and Pattern Recognition’ Area in the Centre for Telecommunications and Multimedia of INESC TEC. His scientific research can be summed up in three major topics: computer vision (image and video analysis and processing), machine learning and decision support systems. Image and video processing focuses on medical images, biometrics and video object tracking for applications such as surveillance and sports. The work on machine learning cares mostly with the adaptation of learning to the challenging conditions presented by visual data. The particular emphasis of the work in decision support systems goes to medical applications, always anchored on the automatic analysis of visual data. Cardoso has co-authored 150+ papers, 50+ of which in international journals.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: Data Science in the wild: the good, the bad and (mostly) the ugly

Part 1: Introduction and Context.

Ambiguity of the term “science”. Spectrum of possibilites. Setting the Expectations Right.

Part 2: The Role of a Science Team.

Complicated vs Complex Systems. The need for a science team. Science vs BA vs Engineering vs Product Management. Local Efficiency vs Global Resilience. Entropy Reduction. Failing Fast Strategies. Force Multipliers. Knowledge diffusion and fertilization.

Part 3: Building a Science Team.

Ability to Adapt. Generalists and JIT Specialization. Growing organically vs growing via Fertilization. Managing Size, Process and Speed. Team Mitosis. Building a team - analogy products vs brands.

Part 4: Common pitfalls.

The myth that everything is a science problem. The dangers of vertical organizations: products vs capabilities. Reductionism vs Contamination. Processes: Things Right vs Right Things. The dangers of the invisible wins. Getting fat.

Part 5: Unsolicited Advice.

Building your own process. Focusing on capabilities. Finding engineering support. Communication, Prototyping and Inspiration. Exporting ownership. Learning and Teaching.

Short Bio:
Luis Sarmento holds a PhD in Computer Science from University of Porto (2010), with background in Electrical Engineering (Bs+MsC) and Artificial Intelligence (MsC). He has been working in the fields of Natural Language / Search for about 15 years, both as a member of research groups at the University of Porto and in industry. In 2010 he joined Portugal Telecom / SAPO as tech lead for Big-Data and Recommender Systems, and in 2012 he joined Amazon to lead research teams in the fields of Query Understanding and Voice Shopping.

In 2017, and after 7 years in industry, Luis is taking a sabbatical year to study topics located in the interface between Machine Learning and Abstract Algebra. He will also be writing in his blog ( https://imaginaryaxis.wordpress.com/ ) about random topics in mathematics and he will be practicing his futurologist skills by speculating about AI in his LinkedIn feed.

title: DSPT #5 - Data Science at Scale in Telcos
description:
2017, the perfect year to be an unicorn.... or not! 😎

The fifth meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/groups/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Wednesday, 18th January, 2017, 18h30, at IEUA (https://www.ua.pt/ieua/) (Incubadora de Empresas da Universidade de Aveiro).

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: “Forecasting the capacity of cellular networks” with João Bastos, Data Scientist at Nokia.

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2: "Smart Cloud of Things: an evolved IoT platform for Telco providers" with Mário Antunes, researcher for the Advanced Telecommunications and Networks Group at IT-Aveiro.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking (place TBA)

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there! 😎

-------------------------------------

Talk1: “Forecasting the capacity of cellular networks”

The optimization of cellular network capacity usage is an essential operation to promote positive returns on network investments, prevent capacity bottlenecks, and deliver good end user experience. In this talk, I analyze the performance of several statistical models to predict capacity metrics and traffic trends of Radio Network Controllers in a 3G network. I also examine the forecasts generated by combinations of these models.

Short Bio:
João is a Data Scientist at Nokia. Previously, he was a lecturer at the School of Economics and Management of the University of Lisbon (ISEG), post-doctoral fellow at the Laboratory of Instrumentation and Experimental Particle Physics (LIP), and visiting fellow at the European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN) and at the Deutsch Elektronen-Synchrotron (DESY). João holds a PhD in Physics from the University of Coimbra.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: “Smart Cloud of Things: an evolved IoT platform for Telco providers”

IT Aveiro have been heavily invested in IoT and M2M technology. Not only in the communication layer but also in analytic potential of these technologies (from collecting data, processing information and visualization). Although many environments are powered by smart solutions, end users do not have a simple way to gather their collective knowledge and program’s device’s behaviour. Furthermore telecommunication providers wish to act as facilitators, but lack proper components for enabling services over their networks. SCoT (Smart Cloud of Things) is the answer develop by IT Aveiro. It as developed in close collaboration with telecommunication providers. The architecture builds upon ETSI standards, combined with a rich service execution environment, providing facilitated orchestration of services and devices, as well as integrated portals for end users access. It was successfully deployed in 3 scenarios: green house monitoring, smart farming and porthole detection.

Short Bio:
Mário Antunes received his computer and telematics engineering M.Sc. degree in 2011, with first class honors, from the Electronics, Telecommunication and Informatics Department, University of Aveiro, Portugal. He is currently working as a researcher for the Advanced Telecommunications and Networks Group at IT-Aveiro. His main research areas focus on Knowledge Extraction and Context Storage in Internet of Things (IoT) Scenarios using Machine Learning techniques and Big Data repositories. His works include developing efficient ways to deal with unstructured information. These techniques are being implemented and evaluated in advance machine-to-machine projects, such as APOLLO and SCOT.
