title: DSPT #4 - From Natural Language BI to Data Driven Solutions
description:
Hello everyone, we are happy to announce a "Before" Christmas Special Edition of our Meetups.

For this edition, that is going to take place on Tuesday, 13th December 2016, 18h30, at UPTEC - Polo Tecnológico (http://uptec.up.pt/en) (auditorium) we have:

• 18:30-19:00 Welcome and get together (we will try to have something else besides minis).

• 19:00-19:30 Talk “Natural Language BI - challenges of building a new approach to an old industry” with Bruno Antunes, Co-founder and CTO at Wizdee (http://wizdee.com) with a PhD in Artificial Intelligence from FCTUC.

• 19:45-20:00 Networking / Coffee Break / Suggestions.

• 20:00-20:30 Talk “Data Driven Solutions at Farfetch” with Ricardo Sousa, a Data Scientist at Farfetch (http://www.farfetch.com), with a PhD on Machine Learning from University of Porto.

• 20:45 Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00 Dinner will be our Christmas Celebration, so you must come to meet everyone (p.s. it might be an excellent opportunity for networking). Please register here:

http://doodle.com/poll/sx7g2vc9xr37t749

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com.

See you there!

---

Talk 1: Natural Language BI - challenges of building a new approach to an old industry

Abstract:

In Wizdee we believe that democratizing access to data increases companies’ collective intelligence and adds value to individual contributions. That’s why we are a fundamentally different approach to Business Intelligence, one that allows users to explore data by themselves, using everyday language, anytime and anywhere. With Wizdee you just ask your questions and get beautiful visualisations, no need for technical skills, hours of training or obsolete user interfaces. Behind the scenes we use various Artificial Intelligence approaches, including Natural Language Processing, Genetic Algorithms and Case Based Reasoning, to make this possible. In this talk we will give you an overview of the different techniques we have put together to build a product that transforms Business Intelligence into something as easy as using Google.

About Bruno Antunes:

Bruno Antunes is the Co-founder and CTO at Wizdee, a natural language business intelligence platform. He has a PhD in Artificial Intelligence from FCTUC and has previously worked as researcher at the Centre for Informatics and Systems of the University of Coimbra in the fields of Artificial Intelligence, Semantic Web and Knowledge Management.

---

Talk 2: Data Driven Solutions at Farfetch

Abstract:

What is machine learning and artificial intelligence? What has this to do with luxury e-commerce? How to delve into complex models without losing focus? In this talk, I will present the responsibilities of a Data Scientist at Farfetch and some of the undergoing projects such as search, ranking, and recommendations.

About Ricardo Sousa:

Ricardo Sousa is a data scientist at Farfetch, a luxury e-commerce, working on new automatic intelligent systems to augment customer experience at Farfetch website. He worked at INEB where he developed Deep Neural Networks for Transfer Learning with applications on Biomedical data. He did his Ph.D. on Machine Learning at University of Porto and MSc degree in Mathematical Engineering and graduated in Computer Science at School of Sciences of the University of Porto.He collaborates with Vision Computing and Machine Intelligence group members and works as a mentor for undergraduate students in the bio_* Group of the School of Engineering of the University of Porto.From 2013-2015 he was one of the organizers of the VISUM Summer School.

title: DSPT #3 - Is Big Data just a buzzword?
description:
And we’re back!

The third meetup of Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/groups/datascienceportugal) is going to take place on Tuesday, 29th November, 2016, 18h30, at IEUA (https://www.ua.pt/ieua/) (Incubadora de Empresas da Universidade de Aveiro).

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together.

• 19:00-19:30: Talk 1: "DataOps - The next level" with Filipe Coelho, PhD in Computer Science from FEUP, Senior Data Scientist at Veniam (https://veniam.com/).

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Coffee break.

• 20:00-20:30: Talk 2: “Fraud Prevention with Machine Learning” with João António, Senior Data Engineer at Feedzai (https://feedzai.com/), Master Degree from University of Coimbra.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking (place TBA)

Do you want to be a sponsor in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

-------------------------------------

Talk1: "DataOps - The next level"

Now that the all powerful Data Science Unicorn(tm) is seen both as a myth and a fallacy, it's time to embrace Data Science as a mixture of both Product and Engineering skills, Analytics and Data Storage platforms, Machine Learning and Visualization. As DevOps became the mindset of modern and scalable technology companies, so must the DataOps mindset become the core of data-driven teams and startups.

Short Bio: Filipe Coelho, PhD in Computer Science from FEUP, is currently a Senior Data Scientist at Veniam. Previously, he was Lead Data Scientist / Researcher at SAPO - Portugal Telecom & INESC TEC, and Big Data Engineer at Farfetch.

-------------------------------------

Talk2: “Fraud Prevention with Machine Learning”

"Feedzai makes commerce safe for business customers and creates a better experience for their consumers through machine learning. Financial services companies use Feedzai’s anti-fraud technology to keep commerce moving safely, amounting to over 2 billion dollars analyzed per day with 99.999% availability. All this is possible using a mix of proprietary software; big data technologies such as Cassandra, Spark and Hadoop; machine learning algorithms either from current literature or researched in-house; and human insight and intuition in the fraud prevention field. On this talk we'll give you an overview on how all these aspects were put together and some of the biggest challenges they helped us solve.

Short Bio: João António is a Senior Data Engineer at Feedzai, with a Master Degree from University of Coimbra and over 6 years of experience in the Telco and Finance fields. Currently part of Feedzai's Research and Data Science team, his career includes roles as Software Engineer, Delivery Engineer, Data Engineer and Data Scientist."

title: DSPT #2 - Data Science Portugal is coming to Porto
description:
Data Science Portugal (https://www.facebook.com/groups/datascienceportugal) meetups are coming to Porto!

The event is going to take place on Thursday, 3rd November, 2016, 18h30, at UPTEC - Polo Tecnológico (http://uptec.up.pt/) (auditorium).

The preliminary agenda for the meetup is the following:

• 18:30-19:00: Welcome and get together (don’t forget your input in the whiteboard).

• 19:00-19:30: Talk “The Python Way: predicting entity popularity on Twitter” with Pedro Saleiro, a PhD student in Informatics Engineering at FEUP and researcher at SAPO Labs UP (http://labs.sapo.pt/) (worked in FCT funded projects such as POPSTAR (http://popstar.pt/) and REACTION (http://dmir.inesc-id.pt/project/Reaction)).

• 19:45-20:00: Networking / Whiteboard review (group involvement).

• 20:00-20:30: Talk “Data Streams Processing at Betfair with Storm (http://storm.apache.org/) & Kafka (https://kafka.apache.org/)” with Miguel Veiga, a Senior Backend Developer at Blip (http://www.blip.pt/), Master's Degree in Informatics from Minho University, and responsible for the design, development and maintenance of Betfair’s Feeds Platform.

• 20:45: Closing, hanging out and some beers

• 21:00: Dinner is optional but it might be an excellent opportunity for networking (place TBA)

Do you what to give a talk in future meetups? Please contact us to info@datascienceportugal.com

See you there!

---

Talk 1: The Python Way: predicting entity popularity on Twitter

Abstract:

import pymongo

import numpy

import matplotlib.pyplot

import pandas.Series

import pandas.DataFrame

import sklearn.feature_extraction

import sklearn.preprocessing

import sklearn.decomposition

import sklearn.svm

import sklearn.linear_model

import sklearn.ensemble

import sklearn.metrics

import lda

import pickle

Abstract v2 (in case you’re not a pythonist):

This talk will cover a Data Science project using Python from end to end. The project goal consists in predicting entity popularity on Twitter based on content features extracted from the news. We will discuss fetching data from a NoSQL database, time series generation and analysis using Pandas, text based feature extraction and vectorizing using scikit-learn, supervised learning, evaluation and deployment in Python.

We run several experiments using a dataset of over 150M tweets and 5M news.

About Pedro Saleiro:

Pedro Saleiro is a PhD student in Informatics Engineering at FEUP under supervision of Prof. Carlos Soares and Prof. Eduarda Mendes Rodrigues. His research interests consist in Data Mining, Information Extraction, Information Retrieval, Machine Learning, Social Computing and related AI fields.

Since 2012, he is a researcher at SAPO Labs UP and has worked in FCT funded projects such as POPSTAR and REACTION. In 2014, he did an internship at Microsoft Research in Cambridge where he worked in query expansion and entity-relationship search with Natasa Milic-Frayling. From 2009 to 2012, he was a researcher at Fraunhofer Portugal working in EU projects related with information processing and remote management for e-health.

Talk 2: Data Streams Processing at Betfair with Storm & Kafka

Abstract:

The integration of different feed data providers drove Betfair to create a streams processing platform capable of ingesting, transforming, and pushing high volumes of data into the Betfair ecosystem. This platform was designed in the order to be consistently responsive, resilient and scalable. This was achieved through the use of technologies like Apache Storm (distributed real-time stream processing engine), and Apache Kafka (distributed messaging system that provides a strong durability and fault tolerance guarantees). Today, Feeds Platform is one of the key pieces on the event and market management flow, enabling Betfair to increase the number of offering events to their Exchange and Sportsbook customers.

About Miguel Veiga:

Miguel Veiga is a Senior Backend Developer at Blip, with a Master's Degree in Informatics from Minho University. He’s currently responsible for the design, development and maintenance of Betfair’s Feeds Platform

title: DSPT #1 - Kick-off and Python Data Science Environment Workshop
description:
And let the games begin! :D

This first workshop will serve as a kick-off for the divulgation of the Data Science Portugal Community that is based in Portugal.

We will start by presenting the project, the organization team and to incentivate the community members to present themselfs. Then we will present a small workshop on how to build your personnal python data science environment on your local machine.

😎

----

Este 1º meetup servirá como pontapé de arranque para a criação da comunidade ligada à área de Data Science em Portugal.

Haverá espaço para apresentação e visão do projeto, da equipa organizadora, apresentação de todos os participantes e ainda será dado um pequeno workshop de introdução ao ambiente de Python para tarefas relacionadas com Data Science.

Agenda:

1. Apresentação inicial
2. Workshop “Introdução a Data Science em Python” com Rui Mendes, Data Analyst do projeto GoWi-Fi e entusiasta de Data Science.
3. Espaço para café e networking
4. Considerações finais
5. Jantar livre

Requisitos para o Workshop:
Portátil com a aplicação Conda instalada (http://conda.pydata.org/docs/) e git (opcional).

😎
